## [TO/CC] 2026-04-06 13:02 | Y2|W7 Product Team Weekly Update: What You Need to Know this Week 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Team,

 

Here's What You Need to Know this Week

 

What We're Building — Accelerating Quality of Life Enhancements

 

At our Q4 Town Hall in February, we aligned on a clear priority: Accelerate Quality of Life Enhancements to Fuel our Reference Customer Strategy Globally.

 

In the UK, MKS stands out as a must-win reference customer. The largest Moore International member firm in the country, they joined our January roadmap check-in and flagged an important gap: required Lockdown functionality. Without it, users lacked safeguards against signing off on engagements with unreviewed documents or uncleared issues.

 

We committed to delivering by early May. The team “locked-in” and Lockdown shipped in March — ahead of plan.

 

This is how we build trust with strategic customers: listen closely, communicate with clarity, and deliver with urgency.  Great work, team!

 

How We're Building — Accelerating Our Claude Capabilities

 

One theme I've been emphasizing in my Weekly Note recently: Let’s all get hands-on with Claude Code. 

 

Why? 

 

Because in my direct experience, leveraging Claude Code has changed how I work — fundamentally — in just a handful of weeks. 

 

To lead in AI, we must lead from the front.  So let's all get our hands dirty!

 

Last week, I moved from working in the Claude app to working directly in my computer’s terminal.  The unlock: parallelization. Switching tabs while Claude is processing means I get to keep working, and come back to the results when they’re ready. 

 

Zero waiting.

 

Try it in three steps:

 

1.      Open Terminal 

2.      Type claude and hit Enter 

3.      Start a task — then open a new tab while it runs

 

What are you doing this week to advance your AI-first craft? 

 

Strategy & AI — New Senior Designer Skill for Claude Code

 

And speaking of accelerating your craft via Claude Code — thank you, Alexa Tang, for shipping our new Senior Designer Skill last week!

 

Here's what it does: load it into Clau...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-04-02 11:42 | Re: Caseware audit file lockdowns
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Sent Items

```
Hi Ricky, 

There was an issue that should have been fixed as of Monday, March 23th - 5pm ET with the release of an se update so if the file locked down after that date 24th or later then there is still an issue so we would need the file url and to go through support so we can raise a DIST ticket to platform on the issue.

It should essentially lock down on that 60th day at 11.59pm and before it was doing it at 12 so was technically the next day hence the 61 days issue. Below is the note from the release notes. 

*	
	The automatic lockdown time for an engagement aligns with the lockdown date set and occurs at 11:59 p.m. on that day. The time is based on the time zone of the user who sets the date.

Kind regards 

Liam Bond

Product Owner

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-30 12:01 | Y2 | W6 Product Team Update: What You Need to Know this Week 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Team,

 

Here's What You Need to Know this Week

 

What We're Building — AiDA Aware & DCA Beta Launch

 

This Tuesday, AiDA Aware and Disclosure Checklist Agent (DCA) enter Beta.

 

Both products are being built "global by design", with AiDA Aware already tested internally across 6 regions in English, French, Dutch, Danish, and Spanish — thank you to our global team for leaning into testing & iteration. Initial feedback on both products has been strong. Frazier Deeter, our DCA golden dataset partner, found that even in alpha, DCA cuts disclosure completion time by 50%+.

 

Our global firm relationships are a massive competitive advantage for Caseware. Firms including Citrin Cooperman, Baker Tilly, EisnerAmper, Wipfli, BTI International, Shaw Gibbs, and MNP are bringing multiple stakeholders into the betas, coordinating schedules during peak season, and testing inside real engagement workflows. Building with velocity in partnership with our customers is how we win the agentic future for our customers. More to come!

 

How We're Building — A New Way to Build

 

We have set a bold goal: Launch an integrated, agentic PBC experience into GA on September 15.

 

How long would it take us to build this product using our current product development approach? Twelve to eighteen months. So we've pivoted. We're prototyping requirements (as markdown files), and rapidly iterating through customer discover. We’re passing off requirements to XD as code. And XD is pivoting to a Claude Code-centric approach to design, further accelerating the handoff to Dev. Thank you, Sri Maryada, Alexa Tang and Luis Bravo Anato for driving here. And thank you, Kyle Silverman, for leading. See here <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/personal/srihitha_maryada_caseware_com/_layouts/15/stream.aspx?id=%2fpersonal%2fsrihitha_maryada_caseware_com%2fDocuments%2fMicrosoft+Teams+Chat+Files%2fProduct_Prototype(Sri).mov&referrer=StreamWebApp.Web&referrerScenario=AddressBarCopied.view.ec20fb52...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-27 21:03 | Inside the Studio - Week 30 | Building the Machine, Not Just the Parts
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
There's a phase in every AI initiative where the individual pieces start working — the agent responds, the customer validates, the roadmap takes shape — but they're still separate. Separate teams, separate backlogs, separate conversations. The risk isn't that any one piece fails. It's that they never connect.
This week, we connected them. We mapped the full AI ecosystem against our platform capabilities, stack-ranked every opportunity by real impact, and confirmed that all items are either unblocked or will be by the time delivery requires them. That's a statement we couldn't make two weeks ago. We're not building one thing — we're building a system.
And every decision we make now shapes what's possible six months and six years from now.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

We're close to a working Document Intelligence agent
We've been building and testing Document Intelligence outside our current stack — intentionally — to move fast, iterate on prompting, and learn what works without waiting on infrastructure. Now, for the first time, we're bringing all of those learnings inside. The dedicated agent landed this week and is being configured for its first real test this weekend. Everything we've learned over weeks of hands-on work is being carried directly into the production stack. We're close.
The Beta UI is coming together
On the product side, the user interface for Beta is making great progress. The vision is taking shape — an experience that makes AI-powered document extraction feel natural, not bolted on. This is what customers will actually interact with at CWX and beyond.
Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
The ecosystem roadmap is accounted for
The roadmap was restructured into two clear views: platform capabilities with dedicated ownership, and product initiatives with dependencies mapped. This gives every team a shared language for what needs to happen and in what order — and a clear way to flag when som...
```

---

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-27 09:40 | Lee's Goals - Possible Process Improvements
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Good morning. One of my goals for 2025/2026 is to propose possible process improvements. As part of this, I'm proposing the below for the Audit squad. You may well have thought about this already or you may be entirely happy with how things work already, so do with this what you will!

Often Audit squad meetings can go on for much longer than they need to. Members of this team already have numerous other meetings and so this results in time being spent in meetings that could be better spent elsewhere. 

What seems to happen in most of our daily meetings, is one issue crops up that requires input from only a few people that is then discussed at length while the remainder of the squad sits and listens.

Similar things happen during retrospectives and sprint planning meetings. These can be long meetings and often a few people speak at great length about there issues etc while everyone is listening.

All that being said, there is nothing wrong, at all, with finding out some of what happens in other people's days and roles. My hope with this process improvement is simply to raise this and possibly offer one or two suggestions.

Daily meetings
Organisers (Liam/Jonathan) should reinforce each update to be brief, undetailed, just what we've done, what we're going to do and any blockers. If questions arise from these updates, then any discussions to be kept short. Lengthier discussions should be separate meetings.

Retrospective and Audit Planning meetings
Clearer chairing of these meetings needed, to keep things moving and as interesting as possible. 
Would an agenda help?
Explore the possibility of breaking down these bigger meetings into several smaller meetings so that, for instance, QA have one meeting with Liam, content a separate one and so on.

I hope the above is helpful, many thanks.

Lee Hatcher
Senior CaseView developer
lee.hatcher@caseware.com <mailto:lee.hatcher@caseware.com> 
​​​​
 <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fus.content.e...
```

**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

Hi Team, 

  

Here’s What You Need to Know this Week 

  

What We're Building — FY27 Planning. As of March 20, the FY27 Base Product Plan is complete — excluding the FY27 AI Acceleration roadmap, which remains in active discussion and will be finalized over the next three weeks. This week, Product Ops will be reaching out to specific teams as we iterate on high confidence FY27 Product Roadmap delivery milestones. Please make time for these follow-ups — initiative details, effort sizing, and Jira records all need to be complete and accurate before we lock the FY27 Base Plan Product Roadmap by end of week

 

How We're Building — Powered by Claude. I shared a clear message with our PLT last Tuesday: Please invest the time to become proficient with the leading AI tooling. This is our time at Caseware to lead the industry forward. We can only do that if we are proficient with the leading AI tools. Right now, Claude is the obvious winner in enterprise AI tooling. If you don't have access — submit your request here. <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaseware.freshservice.com%2Fsupport%2Fcatalog%2Fitems%2F364&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7Cfac4dd701bab44994d6408de88c82e46%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639098590608669856%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=0jz7W1ajlJkDCtcDJdtP6ZCxe1nRiHQLP%2FkM1f1TZuQ%3D&reserved=0>  If you have any questions, please reach out to Mike Shoss. If you have Claude and are seeking to understand how to use it best, I’ve linked to key learning resources below (YouTube is also a terrific resource).

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-20 21:12 | Inside the Studio - Week 29 | When the Signal Is Clear, You Reorganize Around It
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
Most AI teams spend too long in the validation phase. They run another test, schedule another call, build another deck — because moving from "we think this is right" to "we're restructuring how we work to ship this" is a fundamentally different commitment. It's easy to explore. It's harder to reorganize.
This week, we reorganized. Customer discovery stopped being exploratory and started being confirmatory — firms aren't asking "what is this," they're asking "when can we use it" and "what do you need from us." Internally, we changed how Document Intelligence gets delivered: shorter feedback loops, direct accountability between product and engineering, and a ruthless look at what has to ship versus what can wait. And the FY27 roadmap moved from a sequenced outline to an operating plan with named teams and real dependencies.
None of that happens unless the signal is clear. And the signal is clear.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

Customer discovery is no longer "exploratory" — it's validating our bets
Two discovery calls this week — Withum and Clark Nuber — and both confirmed the same thing: the Excel-first, question-based extraction approach we chose for Document Intelligence is exactly right.
Withum identified 30+ use cases beyond leasing — trust agreements, fair value instruments, SaaS revenue recognition, agent vs. principal determinations, ASC 315 fraud risk. They confirmed beta participation and offered to share firm data under NDA to improve prompting quality. That's not polite interest — that's a firm leaning in.
Clark Nuber has been on DataSnipper for 10 years and they love it — but they see Extractly as "the same product with AI on top." Their biggest question is pricing structure, and their QC Director is attending CWX and our Customer Advisory Board. That's a closing window, not a discovery call.
CWX is getting real
I will be joining a session alongside Kelton on Document Intelligence at CWX in May. The format, scope, and ma...
```

Your Time Off was Approved

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-16 11:30 | Y2 | W4 Product Team Update: What You Need to Know this Week 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Team,

 

Here’s What You Need to Know this Week

 

What We're Building — Q4 Execution.  We reviewed our 16 FY26 Q4 Input Goals as a cross-functional R&D team on March 11. We are currently On-Track across Q4 deliveries, with 15 of 16 input goals reporting as Green (translating into 108 of 117 initiatives progressing as committed), and one input goal reporting as Yellow (State & Local Government delivery), with the team driving to a Path to Green by end-of-week (Prashant is driver). Double clicking on our Top Q4 Priorities — Cloud Performance, CWX AI Launches, Baker Tilly Execution, Extractly Adoption, and Driving a Culture of Quality — we are Green. At the same time, given tight execution timelines, our margin for error is limited.  So let's lean-in together as we enter the second half of Q4, and Finish Strong.

 

How We’re Building — Powered by Claude. We now have 43 members of the Product team leveraging Claude Chat, Claude Cowork, and Claude Code in our day-to-day work — driving truly impressive productivity gains. For example, Claude for PowerPoint is saving us hours by eliminating the manual work of slide creation, editing, and adjustment. Imagine what consistent, high-proficiency usage of Claude across our entire team allows us to achieve week-over-week, quarter-over-quarter. I'd encourage every one of you to keep pushing your Claude fluency. I also want to extend a huge thank you to Mark Kets for his incredible support and leadership in driving this rollout. Thank you, Mark!

 

Strategy & AI — Document Intelligence Alpha for CWX.  Document Intelligence is our in-development Ecosystem-level AI capability.  When we launch Document Intelligence into Alpha at CWX, firms will be able to use agentic capabilities through an Excel-based experience powered by Extractly to extract and derive information from documents, with cloud processing linked directly to the engagement. This includes not only capturing fields that are explicitly stated, but also determining ...

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-13 22:02 | Inside the Studio - Week 28 | From Prototypes to Platform Flow
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
This week was about making our momentum repeatable: clearer portfolio alignment, sharper alpha intent, and a stronger bridge between fast Product-led prototyping and what Engineering can confidently operationalize.
We’re not just building clever demos — we’re shaping the flow of a single platform customers will want to live in.

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

Document Intelligence is setting us up for a strong Alpha signal
Next week we’ll have the API access we need to set up the first Alpha test properly — and that test has a very specific purpose: validate the agent configuration end-to-end to ensure it’s returning the right results, then iterate quickly to tune and improve it based on what we learn.
This isn’t “ship and hope.” It’s a disciplined loop: configure → validate → refine → repeat — until we’ve got a foundation we can trust under real audit pressure.
One coordinated portfolio — so the platform feels like one workflow
Alongside Document Intelligence progress, we’ve continued tightening how Document Intelligence, PBC, and Validate move together. The goal isn’t just alignment on paper — it’s ensuring the “bits and pieces” don’t merely connect… they flow.
That flow is the platform story: customers shouldn’t have to jump between disconnected tools. They should be able to log into one Caseware ecosystem where PBC (intake), intelligence, and validation operate as a connected triangle — each informing and strengthening the others so work moves seamlessly with the right support at the right moment.
Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
Bridging prototype velocity into engineering reality (without slowing down)
A big upcoming internal focus right now is making sure our pace compounds: Product can prototype aggressively, validate the story early, and then we have a clear, intentional path to hand off what’s working into Engineering in a way that fits the real product structure.
Next week, we’re not “building i...
```

## [FROM] 2026-03-13 17:28 | Re: CW Cloud Support
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Inbox

```
Hi Jack

I think I have misunderstood the issue in the past.

I thought WR Partners' issue was the same as that of Dains, in that there was a need for lead schedules in Academy.

Yusuf has completed the specifications for the Academy Lead Schedules.

Liam is in the process of creating the Content Authoring cards to create those Lead Schedules from those specifications and we will be adding those to our next couple of sprints so that the authoring of those cards is targeted to be complete by the end of April.

We will then release them in the Academy template when it is ready following the release of Mercia's update for the AAD 2025/26 (that AAD hasn't been published yet, so we do not have that from Mercia at the moment, but would hope we have it towards the end of April or into early May).

The issue with the Preliminary Tailoring B23 document, which shows Analytic Schedule-type graphs etc, is a lot harder to determine, specify and sort out.
Liam is looking at an AI tool that Valentin is currently working on, which produces a dashboard for these Analytic-type results. It may be that we can link the B23 to that as a solution, but I don't actually have a time-line for that at the moment.

We anticipate the release of the template will be in June, as per our usual schedule, so we will see what we can do regarding the B23 by then.

Jonathan Millar ACA BSc.(Hons.)

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-09 12:09 | Y2 | W3 Product Team Update: What You Need to Know this Week 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Team,

 

Here’s What You Need to Know this Week

 

What We're Building — Strategic Accounts Progress:

Last week, we had Roadmap check-ins with several key strategic accounts, including MNP, EY and Baker Tilly.  With MNP, we reviewed our AI strategy (“This definitely resonates”) and our Future State UX Vision (“This is exciting work. Can we book a follow-up deep dive?”). With EY, we shared how we had delivered to expectation on our FY26 roadmap commitments (“Thank you”), and reviewed our FY27 roadmap commitments — providing early insight into the key enablers we are prioritizing, and setting expectations early on those that we are not (“We appreciate the transparency and partnership.”) Finally, we had a highly successful AI Interoperability Deep Dive with Baker Tilly leadership, setting the stage for an in-person Baker Tilly CEO Review on March 16. Thank you to everyone who supported these important customer touchpoints. Designing & building our products through a customer lens is how we win for them.

 

How We Build — Forward-Deployed Product Leadership for Baker Tilly: 

Over the next two months, Prashant Shashikumar will step into a forward-deployed Product Manager role with Baker Tilly to close structural gaps between customer expectations and delivery execution. The focus will be on deeply understanding BT’s pain points, surfacing systemic risks early, streamlining internal processes, tightening Caseware ↔ AICPA dependency handoffs for Baker Tilly specifically, and aligning production-ready commitments.  The forward deployed model has driven seismic customer value acceleration for companies like Palantir and Stripe.  Our hypothesis is that it can drive meaningful value acceleration for Baker Tilly — increasing clarity, speed, and trust in execution. Thank you, Prashant, for leaning-in to this forward deployed Product Manager experiment!

 

How We Build — FY27 Planning:

This Friday marks a key milestone as we look to finalize FY27 roadmap effort sizing a...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-06 21:59 | Inside the Studio - Week 27 | We Showed the Future and Customers Leaned In
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
This week had real “we’re onto something” energy.
We ran our first customer-facing demo of what we’re building toward for CWX Beta — and the reaction wasn’t polite curiosity. It was pull. The kind of pull where customers lean in and start talking about how they want their teams using this in audits, how fast we can expand coverage, and how badly they want to consolidate scattered tools into one cohesive Caseware ecosystem.

And the best part: this wasn’t a polished product illusion. We proved the story with speed — using prototypes built in Replit and designed in Figma — because velocity is the advantage right now. The teams that win won’t be the ones who talk the biggest. They’ll be the ones who can show the future, validate it with customers, and then ship it in a way that holds up under real audit pressure.

That’s exactly what we’re doing.

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

We’re building with customers and AI in the loop
The customer message came through loud and clear: they want one platform, one workflow, one place where audit work happens without bouncing between disconnected tools.
They also highlighted a key gap they see with a major competitor to Extractly: it struggles with certain real-world document edge cases (where the “next instance” of an entity breaks down). That’s the kind of practical, gritty workflow truth that doesn’t show up in a requirements doc — it shows up when you put something real in front of a customer.
We may not solve every one of these cases inside CWX Beta scope — but this is exactly the kind of workflow we’re designing toward as we expand. The point of these calls is to make sure the roadmap is anchored in real needs, not internal assumptions.
What happens next
Over the next couple of weeks, we’ll keep running these sessions to bring customers closer, tighten the requirements, and turn interest into a co-build cohort that shapes alpha and CWX Beta with us.
If you have customers who you believe woul...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-03-02 12:56 | Y2 | W2 Product Team Update: What You Need to Know this Week 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Team,

 

Here’s What You Need to Know this Week

 

*	What We’re Building — Future State UX Design: Later this morning, we’re sharing a First Look of Caseware’s Future-State UX Design with MNP’s Assurance & Technology leadership.  Designed to deliver an AI-centric, seamless, confidence-inspiring experience, today’s First Look with MNP is the product of months of work across our teams. Thank you, Ian and Brendan for your Design leadership, and to all of our Subject Matter Experts for shaping this work so deeply. Want to see it for yourself? We’ll be sharing this First Look at our R&D All Hands next week. See you there!

 

*	How We Build — FY27 Planning: FY27 Planning is on track — 50% and 80% Plan Readiness gates are complete, and we're driving toward 100% by March 20. That final gate means an aligned FY27 Roadmap, confirmed trade-offs, and readiness for Finance and GTM handoff. (See below for more details.) 

 

*	How We Build — In-Quarter Execution: To enhance visibility & rigor, we're sunsetting the bi-weekly Excel-based Input Goal Tracker and moving to monthly Input Goal Reviews in Jira Product Discovery. We’ll kick this new process off this Thursday with our first Monthly Input Goal Review (we’ll socialize the new process with Input Goal owners at that time; see below for more details). 

 

*	Strategy & AI — In-Quarter Execution: We’re on-track to deliver Caseware Agentic Intelligence to our customers at CWX in May.  Thank you, Quinn, for sharing our Alpha release of AiDA Aware (our embedded, engagement aware intelligent assistant) at our AI Showcasing OpMech last Friday! This is a cross-functional effort.  Thanks to everyone across R&D who contributed to giving life to AiDA Aware!  

 

The future is bright.  We know, because we’re building it.

 

Let’s go!

 

Andrew

 

 

How We Build: Guidance from PM OPS

 

This is your centralized source of truth for Product planning, covering our FY27 Product Planning process, Q1 QRP, and Q4 execution.

 

FY27 P...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-27 21:51 | Inside the Studio - Week 26 | Ecosystem-Connected Efficiency
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
Where was Week 25? I was out last Friday — and I’m choosing to believe you all enjoyed the uninterrupted weekend (and maybe missed me just a bit). This update covers Weeks 25 + 26, so there’s a lot we could unpack. I’ll keep it condensed and focused.
The theme across the last two weeks is simple: AI is moving fast and the winners are the teams that turn velocity into a repeatable product story. The market is rapidly pushing AI into the tools people actually use (not just chat windows). Anthropic, for example, is making a strong enterprise push with Claude operating directly across Excel and PowerPoint workflows
That’s the bar. And that’s exactly why our focus has on Ecosystem has been to: Realize Greater Efficiency Through an Ecosystem-Connected Platform.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

One timeline, one language: Alpha → Beta (CWX) → GA
We cleaned up internal confusion this week there were multiple names and timelines floating around depending on who you talked to and while this was on purpose we’re all now aligned on one clear progression:
Alpha: Internal validation
A controlled product environment to test the agent end-to-end with SME's and confirm we’re getting the outcomes we want: quality, consistency, and real workflow acceleration.
Beta: CWX
This is the CWX Beta we’ve been driving toward. Scope has not changed.
Beta is where we validate in real-world conditions with the feedback loops that matter.
GA: General Availability
GA is where “it works” becomes “it’s a product.” Clear readiness gates, supportability, and predictable sequencing so adoption is deliberate, not accidental.
Co-building with customers (and we want more partners)
A key part of how we’ll win here is continuing to build alongside customers, not in isolation. I’ve been collecting feedback from multiple areas across the business, and we’ve already reached out to a set of key customers we want to partner with as we shape this future.
If you have customers who wo...
```

Subject: Recording of audit report date within Caseware

 

Hi all, 

 

The change implemented today to not permit the audit report date entered into files to be dated on any day prior to the current day is a change we were not signposted about and has already caused a number of issues. I’ve reported to support (see attached) and have been told that it is a designed restriction, which doesn’t make sense. We were not informed about this and it does not feature in the release notes <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdownloads.caseware.com%2Fassets%2FPDF%2520Help%2520Site%2F%40Cloud%2520Platform%2F2026-02-Cloud-Engagement-Update.pdf&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C3a51cc540f8748f4055308de7546f6ef%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639077144832075175%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=FekIHWXcFtO8MCHs3%2BRUAhT2wzSJB%2Fcjkxq5TyWXR6Q%3D&reserved=0> .

The change implemented today to not permit the audit report date entered into files to be dated on any day prior to the current day is a change we were not signposted about and has already caused a number of issues. We were not informed about this and it does not feature in the release notes <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdownloads.caseware.com%2Fassets%2FPDF%2520Help%2520Site%2F%40Clo...

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-26 13:36 | Recording of audit report date within Caseware
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi all, 

 

The change implemented today to not permit the audit report date entered into files to be dated on any day prior to the current day is a change we were not signposted about and has already caused a number of issues. I’ve reported to support (see attached) and have been told that it is a designed restriction, which doesn’t make sense. We were not informed about this and it does not feature in the release notes <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdownloads.caseware.com%2Fassets%2FPDF%2520Help%2520Site%2F%40Cloud%2520Platform%2F2026-02-Cloud-Engagement-Update.pdf&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C0810f1fb54c94974ddc408de753be60b%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639077097704916429%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=cKeNgK4%2BJ77ud4IMZHlQ0DzbDRMceewoK%2BlSgdkPO9I%3D&reserved=0> . 

 

While in theory a partner should log into a Caseware file immediately after an audit report is signed and record the date, in practice this may slip for a multitude of reasons. Whether it is that paper copies are signed in person or simply that it had been forgotten. To prevent the accurate recording of the date in the system is unacceptable. I’ve just got out of a meeting where we discussed this and we are all very concerned that the audit file documentation and the file tracking that we get through using the API, which is a fundamental process relied on by our teams for the purpose of file archiving, which as you are aware was a workaround we engineered in the absence of any file oversight. We have spent a lot of time developing this and told the FRC (our regulator) that it will be effective. It will leave us in a very difficult position if we cannot get this information.

 

This needs to be resolved ASAP – it’s not like the issues we discussed last week, it’s absolutely fundamental. 

 

Kind regards,

 

Tim

 

TIM GONZAGA...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-23 12:23 | Y2 | W1 Product Team Update: Today Is Day One
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

A year ago, I joined you as CPO. 

 

Thank you for the discipline, ownership, and velocity you’ve brought to building The global, AI-powered platform of choice for accounting professionals. 

 

Year One was about building Strategic Clarity, Operating Discipline, Platform Foundation, Enterprise Credibility, and AI Momentum to position Caseware to lead. 

 

Year Two is about leading boldly — stepping forward with confidence and ambition as we scale enterprise cloud migration, accelerate global adoption, and define governed, domain-led AI across our platform and ecosystem.

 

We will approach it with a Day One mindset. 

 

Day One means customer obsession. 

Day One means high standards. 

Day One means bold experimentation. 

Day One means urgency. 

 

One year ago, I wrote that we have the opportunity — and responsibility — to lead the transformation of assurance & financial reporting. 

 

That is even more true today. 

 

Our vision remains: Power trust in the global economy. 

Our strategy remains: Be the global, AI-powered platform of choice for accounting professionals. 

 

Today is Day One. 

 

Let’s go. 

— Andrew 

 

 

Andrew Smith

​​​​

Chief Product Officer

	
andrew.smith@caseware.com <mailto:andrew.smith@caseware.com> 

 

```

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-17 13:25 | Week 52 Product Team Update: What You Need to Know This Week
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

What You Need to Know this Week

 

*	Busy Season Readiness: Through busy season, our Smart Engagement Team is monitoring system health and capacity daily. Current data show platform performance is healthy, with ample headroom in capacity.  This is mission critical work.

 

	*	Special thanks to the SE Daily Ops Review team: Dirk Bach, Zak Mandel, Jair Tamayo, Bogdan Craciun, Andrei Resmerita, Anna Levitskaia, Kyle Good, FanFan Huang, Yehor Kravchenko, Tom Cassiotis, Ed Tan, Brian Chaparro, and Nicolas Rivera.

 

*	Cloud Financials in Denmark: Last week, we launched a major update to Cloud Financials in Denmark, improving both efficiency and quality in one of the most detail-heavy workflows in financial reporting. To learn more, please head over to Tine Bünger’s excellent Launch Post in Caseware LaunchPad: [link HERE <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fteams.microsoft.com%2Fl%2Fmessage%2F19%3Aa250db11aeab41708a23783ede5c1cff%40thread.tacv2%2F1770753670950%3FtenantId%3D7b8a41e4-2f76-4eef-87d3-f75dd19c0e56%26groupId%3Da7f28144-050c-46ef-80ac-6861e94b6cbb%26parentMessageId%3D1770753670950%26teamName%3DOne%2520Caseware%2520Team%26channelName%3DCaseware%2520LaunchPad%26createdTime%3D1770753670950&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C01eff6fafc484e2658eb08de6e27cdd9%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639069315350060427%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=1qk6ECWLzJzdkBcRqWLv%2FGTl86AhWxlYmNVgoXVRjiw%3D&reserved=0> ]. 

 

	*	Special thanks to Terina Kallehave, Emma Sarvegaard, Morten Landtved, Andre Sunke, Bruce Bolton, Tine Bünger, and Don van Zijtveld.

 

*	FY27 Planning Update: This week, we are driving toward our Feb 20 > 50% Plan Readiness gate.

 

	*	By Feb 20, teams must:

		*	Ensure all initiatives are mapped to Input Goals in Jira.
		*	Enter initial planned effort (in squad sprints) at the initiative level ...

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-13 21:37 | Inside the Studio - Week 24 | Co-Build the Future (Cloud Customers Wanted)
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
This week we did the work that separates teams who talk about speed from teams who generate it: alignment with teeth. We tightened CWX readiness through real cross-team collaboration, and we pushed FY27 planning into the phase that matters—sequencing. Strategy isn’t the document; it’s the order of operations. Get sequencing right and Automation, AI, and agentic capability stop being buzzwords and start becoming an advantage the market can’t catch.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

Document Intelligence Agent:

	Upload → relevant facts surfaced → validate in Excel → act faster

We’re putting real weight behind building and launching a Document Intelligence Agent with customers. The value is intentionally concrete: when a file is uploaded, it identifies and surfaces the relevant information—so teams can review, validate, and act faster in Excel without manual searching.
This isn’t a “build it and hope” release. The plan is to partner and co-build with a small set of Cloud customers so we shape the workflow around real evidence review, real constraints, and real trust requirements from day one.
Co-build Call to Action (starting next week)
If you can connect us to Cloud customers who want to partner early, send them my way. We’re looking for customers who:

*	Are on Cloud (required)

*	Use Extractly or don’t (both are valuable)

*	Have heavy document workflows where time-to-answer matters

*	Bonus: they currently use another tool (competitive displacement opportunity)

What we need from you

*	Customer name + primary contact

*	A one-liner on their workflow pain (“where the time gets burned today”)

*	Whether they’re currently using Extractly / a competitor / manual process

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast

Most of this week was spent in FY27 planning. We’re defining outcomes and sequencing the work so it’s buildable, adoptable, and supportable—refinement stays open through the month, but the shape is now clear....
```

From: Mitchell Payne <mitchell.payne@caseware.com>
Sent: 12 February 2026 11:20
To: Jonathan Millar <jonathan.millar@caseware.com>; Naqib Daud <naqib.daud@caseware.com>
Cc: Jack Holloway <jack.holloway@caseware.com>; Liam Bond <liam.bond@caseware.com>
Subject: Re: Confirmation on TB Live Sync Mapping Behaviour (Cloud Financials to Cloud Audit) 
 
Hi all,

Yes, the described behaviour is correct to our expectations.

As the mapping from Financials to the Audit required certain amounts of interpretation and decision making due to their differences, it was deemed essential that user be able to remap in the Audit side, in case there was disagreement with our allocations. Otherwise, they would be fully "locked" into whatever we configured.

This means the mapping has to be a one-time process at the point of connection. Otherwise any mapping corrections on the audit  side would be undone.

Let me know if you need anything else,

Cheers,

Mitchell Payne
Product Owner - Cloud and Analytics

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-12 11:20 | Re: Confirmation on TB Live Sync Mapping Behaviour (Cloud Financials to Cloud Audit)
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi all,

Yes, the described behaviour is correct to our expectations.

As the mapping from Financials to the Audit required certain amounts of interpretation and decision making due to their differences, it was deemed essential that user be able to remap in the Audit side, in case there was disagreement with our allocations. Otherwise, they would be fully "locked" into whatever we configured.

This means the mapping has to be a one-time process at the point of connection. Otherwise any mapping corrections on the audit  side would be undone.

Let me know if you need anything else,

Cheers,

Mitchell Payne
Product Owner - Cloud and Analytics
mitchell.payne@caseware.com <mailto:mitchell.payne@caseware.com> 

Caseware UK Ltd. Trading Address: County Gate 2, Staceys Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME14 1ST, United Kingdom

Registered in England, Registered number 03347764. Registered Office, 1 Princeton Mews, 167-169 London Road, Kingston upon Thames, Surrey, KT2 6PT

________________________________

From: Jonathan Millar <jonathan.millar@caseware.com>
Sent: 12 February 2026 10:16
To: Naqib Daud <naqib.daud@caseware.com>; Mitchell Payne <mitchell.payne@caseware.com>
Cc: Jack Holloway <jack.holloway@caseware.com>; Liam Bond <liam.bond@caseware.com>
Subject: Re: Confirmation on TB Live Sync Mapping Behaviour (Cloud Financials to Cloud Audit) 
 
Hi Naqib

I believe that is the case.

Because of how they were created in the first place there are differences in the mappings between the standard Financials and standard Audit mappings (Audit uses dimensions, whereas Financials does not).

Therefore, the syncing of changing in mappings will not occur between the two, as there may not be an equivalent in the audit system. When the sync TB is set up it uses an ini file to map the two TBs to their relevant mappings. Unless that it used again, the system will not know which mapping code to use in the Audit system.

@Mitchell Payne <mailto:mitchell.payne@caseware.com>  may be able to provi...
```

________________________________

From: Jonathan Millar <jonathan.millar@caseware.com>
Sent: 12 February 2026 10:16
To: Naqib Daud <naqib.daud@caseware.com>; Mitchell Payne <mitchell.payne@caseware.com>
Cc: Jack Holloway <jack.holloway@caseware.com>; Liam Bond <liam.bond@caseware.com>
Subject: Re: Confirmation on TB Live Sync Mapping Behaviour (Cloud Financials to Cloud Audit) 
 
Hi Naqib

I believe that is the case.

Because of how they were created in the first place there are differences in the mappings between the standard Financials and standard Audit mappings (Audit uses dimensions, whereas Financials does not).

Therefore, the syncing of changing in mappings will not occur between the two, as there may not be an equivalent in the audit system. When the sync TB is set up it uses an ini file to map the two TBs to their relevant mappings. Unless that it used again, the system will not know which mapping code to use in the Audit system.

@Mitchell Payne <mailto:mitchell.payne@caseware.com>  may be able to provide further explanation on this.

## [FROM] 2026-02-12 10:16 | Re: Confirmation on TB Live Sync Mapping Behaviour (Cloud Financials to Cloud Audit)
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Naqib

I believe that is the case.

Because of how they were created in the first place there are differences in the mappings between the standard Financials and standard Audit mappings (Audit uses dimensions, whereas Financials does not).

Therefore, the syncing of changing in mappings will not occur between the two, as there may not be an equivalent in the audit system. When the sync TB is set up it uses an ini file to map the two TBs to their relevant mappings. Unless that it used again, the system will not know which mapping code to use in the Audit system.

@Mitchell Payne <mailto:mitchell.payne@caseware.com>  may be able to provide further explanation on this.

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-09 13:24 | Week 51 Product Team Update: Q3 Impact & Q4 Roadmap
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

As we accelerate into Q4, I want to share both the impact we delivered in Q3 and where we’re going next.

 

While I’ve included a What You Need to Know This Week summary below, I strongly encourage you to read on. The depth of progress we made in Q3 — and the clarity of what we’re committing to in Q4 — is something this team should be proud of. It’s a clear signal of how we’re building towards our goal of delivering the global, AI-powered platform of choice for accounting professionals, in service of powering trust in the global economy. 

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

 

*	Q3 Delivery: We closed Q3 with meaningful, global progress across Audit, Financials, Platform, and AI — unlocking cloud migration, strengthening enterprise readiness, and dramatically accelerating AI development velocity. 

 

*	How We Build: We’re entering Q4 with a clear operating model — a single source of truth for roadmaps [ here <https://casewareonline.sharepoint.com/:p:/s/AllProduct-Team/IQCzDQzwCtZQRryTO8CHNWdxAaA6U9CT8jMg8PZCz8TxJkE?e=vyuuOX&wdOrigin=TEAMS-MAGLEV.null_ns.rwc&wdExp=TEAMS-TREATMENT&wdhostclicktime=1770561257031&web=1&&sdata=SjJ5MmVLK1JrWDU0MVNZOUJhaW4yNjZPYWVNcVU0OXk4V3V5SzZqbGtqND0%3d> ] and delivery status [here <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaseware.atlassian.net%2Fjira%2Fpolaris%2Fprojects%2FQRP%2Fideas%2Fview%2F10144368&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7Cdd1c591abb464c5c283908de67de9708%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639062402963998343%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=24%2F06RiNYh6co...

## [TO/CC] 2026-02-06 21:57 | Inside the Studio - Week 23 | Reading the signals before they’re obvious
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
This week was all about research and planning—not just for CWX (scope unchanged), but for what comes after. In startup land you learn a “wartime” habit: the world rarely announces the next shift out loud, but it always leaves signals. The pace around us is accelerating, and Studio’s job is to make sure Caseware is early—not reactive—by turning those signals into something real we can test, learn from, and then scale with confidence.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
Post‑CWX Document Intelligence — a tangible preview (directional, not a CWX commitment)
I took a small dose of “what the future could look like” and turned it into a real, touchable slice of post‑CWX Document Intelligence. It’s not what we’re shipping for CWX, and some elements may never ship—but it’s the right kind of artifact: a concrete experiment that forces clarity and gets high-signal feedback fast.
In one flow: you can start an extraction by choosing a reusable template and a set of documents; an agent runs across them to search, extract, compare, summarize, cross‑reference, and do basic calculations; results land in a table with citations; you can add one‑off questions that re-run across all documents, add new documents later and rerun, and export a package (CSV + linked source docs). On the trust side, we’re pushing toward cell‑level explainability (source text + exact PDF location with evidence), inline corrections when AI is wrong or incomplete, the ability to populate cells by highlighting text in the PDF, and a full audit history of changes.
Early client reactions to this direction have been consistently strong—especially around table-first outputs + citations + auditability.
Now it’s your turn to experience it firsthand. The link below lets you explore the direction, interact with the workflow, and see how it behaves in practice.
ShossTract - Powered by Replit <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fbit.ly%2F4ce6hDS&data=05%7C02%7Cli...
```

Hi All,

You will shortly receive the invite for the prioritisation touchpoint call we discussed at last month’s MKS onsite. Please forward if you feel others should be involved.

While the main objective is to understand the initial prioritisation of their most important requests, their updated quality‑of‑life items (sheet 2) is quite extensive. I’d be grateful if you could review these ahead of the call and be ready to share any insight to help make best use of time.

 

The 5 urgent sign‑off and lock‑down items (sheet 1) have already been discussed in detail, and expectations are currently set for an April/May release. I fully expect MKS to ask early in the conversation whether this timeline remains accurate.

 

Thanks in advance for your help and support.

 

Kind regards,

Ricky Mahay

**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
The world isn’t slowing down—AI is pressing the accelerator. We’re treating our near‑ and long‑term arc as a living plan that adapts as reality shifts. Two fresh signals sharpened our path this week: Claude in Excel (beta) <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsupport.claude.com%2Fen%2Farticles%2F12650343-claude-in-excel&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C7acae47be5474033ae7b08de6047c38a%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639054058554302162%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=Ot%2FOpWE6TJ0WNUrDv%2BzZNmdJIc79Qz%2BY2St%2FhhRVodc%3D&reserved=0>  for deeper spreadsheet actions on a user’s behalf, and OpenClaw (prev MoltBot / Clawd Bot) <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fopenclaw.ai%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C7acae47be5474033ae7b08de6047c38a%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639054058554349557%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=zIUv1RS%2BPGnsinGeSdNK95YgxVXdGvFivUs2bWp%2F1KM%3D&reserved=0> , a desktop‑level agent that can operate like a user. The implication is clear: as models get better and cheaper, we’ll bias toward abstractions that let us swap capabilities in without heavy rework. That’s how Studio turns uncertainty into proof—fast.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

*	Prototype, two parts—one story (shared with stakeholders):

	*	
		Working flow that runs end‑to‑end to the desired outcome, and
	*	
		Figma look‑and‑feel standard so the path to product is unmistakable.

*	
	Together, they make the value obvious and the next engineering step concrete.
*	
	Expert validation (LeaseJava): I spent an hour with Mike Chang, the builder of LeaseJava and a leasing domain expert. He was impressed with what’s shipping for CWX and where we’re hea...

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-30 13:03 | Re: Thursday 15th January [MKS-ACTIVE.FID5620043]
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Sent Items

```
Hi Everyone,
There are two related items impacting the B40, and I’m looking for help with timelines so I can respond to the client with a complete picture since it affects platform and solutions side in terms of fixes being provided. 
1) B40 performance issue (AIRA SE Builder form)
We’re seeing performance degradation on the B40 due to the AIRA SE Builder form (a cut-down version based on the ART from GCS). This appears to be related to the SE Builder update/migration impacting performance, as noted in the comment here - https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/DIST-65826?focusedCommentId=2206748 <https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/DIST-65826?focusedCommentId=2206748>  

Request: Could you share when this Epic is currently planned / the expected timing for delivery from the platform side? https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/FLAT-12224 <https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/FLAT-12224>  
2) General responsiveness / visibility issue when making selections
Separately, there’s a broader platform issue where visibility/responsiveness is not behaving as expected on the B40 (and other documents) when users make selections. This was raised during UAT and is tracked here:
https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/DIST-66613
Request: Could I get an estimated resolution timeframe for this item as well?
________________________________

What Solutions is doing (mitigation / stop-gap)
On the Solutions side, we’re doing what we can to mitigate the B40 performance issue by:

*	
	Refactoring the form for performance
*	
	Limiting the number of embedded forms rendered at a time (Keegan’s suggestion to render only forms that are visible to the user).

These changes are tracked here:

*	
	https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/UKCAUD-14327
*	
	https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/UKCAUD-14637

This is a mitigation/stop-gap based on the notes in DIST-65826, the issue should be more fully addressed once the platform work lands.
Release plan (Solutions): The Solutions fix is scheduled to go...
```

This is a reference to the Audit Procedures Manual. That is made available to UK Audit Desktop users through Salesforce. However, for Cloud users a link has to be given directly on request by Pete, as it isn't currently possible to automate that from Salesforce, for some reason.

In due course, if we get the permissions and IP sorted we would want this available through AiDA in this area.

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-26 12:59 | Week 49 Product Team Update: What You Need to Know this Week
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

In the spirit of continuous improvement, I’m experimenting with brevity this week. Let me know what you think.

What You Need to Know This Week

1. What We Build — IDEA

IDEA is a powerful and differentiated analytics capability that solves a real, pressing customer problem. As auditors face ever larger and more complex datasets, they need to extract, prepare, and analyze data efficiently without compromising auditability or defensibility. IDEA delivers that combination of scale, transparency, and control in a way general-purpose tools cannot. If you haven’t experienced IDEA recently, I strongly encourage you to  watch this short demo <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcasewareonline-my.sharepoint.com%2Fpersonal%2Fharry_simpson_caseware_com%2F_layouts%2F15%2Fstream.aspx%3Fid%3D%252Fpersonal%252Fharry_simpson_caseware_com%252FDocuments%252FAttachments%252FIDEA%2520Overview%2520%2528almost%25205%2520minutes%2529.mp4%26referrer%3DStreamWebApp.Web%26referrerScenario%3DAddressBarCopied.view.09e33a9e-d0e4-4a24-92b5-c2058188943f%26ga%3D1&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C2f3e803374ed488906b508de5cdab8f5%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639050291678886806%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=dG51H09AcqJhslPbWMEDY1m%2B6Vw33cP1NH0iq27bbbs%3D&reserved=0>  (thank you, Harry Simpson!) — it’s a concrete reminder of why we are recognized as a global leader in audit-grade analytics, and why IDEA remains a critical foundation <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/ap/w-59584e83/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcasewareonline-my.sharepoint.com%2F%3Aw%3A%2Fr%2Fpersonal%2Fandrew_smith_caseware_com%2F_layouts%2F15%2FDoc.aspx%3Fsourcedoc%3D%257B1067AE60-9DE3-4C54-9B25-5230EC6A52DA%257D%26file%3DDocument%252013.docx%26action%3DeditNew%26mobileredirect%3Dtrue%26wdOrigin%3DTEAMS-MAGLEV.null_ns.rwc%26wdPreviousSession%3D2dfa235b-6fa9-4b0b-90...

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-23 21:47 | Inside the Studio - Week 21 | Diverge → reconverge (Play beats perfection)
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
We deliberately diverged this week—engineering spikes to expose constraints, an updated flow to tighten the user path, and a prototype from me to ground the debate. Early next week we’ll reconverge, merge, and commit—then finish the week with a prototype in hands and all artifacts updated. That’s the Studio mandate: turn uncertainty into proof, fast.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

*	Updated flow: A job‑specific path that keeps people in context and makes accept/override obvious and auditable.

*	User‑intro prototype: Guided first‑run that explains what’s happening and why it’s safe, ready to validate immediately after we reconverge.

*	Build‑once, reuse everywhere: All new rules, tools, and APIs for this capability will live in a central, shared layer—so teams don’t rebuild the same parts twice. If something is added for AiDA (or any other initiative), this generic capability can pick it up—and vice versa. That’s how we avoid duplication and multiply speed.

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast

*	Template Builder (for Document Analysis Agent): If what we need doesn’t exist, we make it—carefully. This simple internal UI produces the engineering‑ready template file required for the generic capability. It is not a customer feature and not a general templating tool; it exists only to support this capability pre‑production.

*	Enable test velocity (now): With IT, I’m spinning up lightweight utilities so I can move fast on testing, then decide what (if anything) to roll into our product lifecycle.

*	Resourcing is locked: Extractly leads; Cloud Platform and DA support. Owners/sequencing will be posted after the reconvergence notes are captured.

Run the Plays | Turning plans into progress

*	Post‑CWX planning: Early next week, we shift into the post‑CWX picture. I’ll continue shaping a near‑term + long‑term roadmap as a living plan that updates with signal and feedback.

*	Next week’s newsletter goal: Share a proto...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-19 14:20 | Week 48 Product Team Update | Authoring Velocity, Key FY27 Themes, Agentic Customer Value 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

 

*	Cloud Visibility Explorer is live — and it’s already accelerating authoring at scale. We shipped a new internal tool that lets authors load a cloud document configuration and instantly trace upstream visibility dependencies, see downstream impact, and auto-detect broken conditions. What used to take hours of manual logic-walking can now be diagnosed in seconds — increasing authoring velocity, reducing template risk, and directly supporting enterprise-grade customization in our cloud platform.  Thank you, Jason Bradley for leading on driving #AI-primed velocity!

 

*	European customers reinforced that Caseware cloud is delivering real value today. After spending last week with firms across the Netherlands and the UK, the message was consistent: customers are realizing meaningful efficiency gains and remain committed to Caseware as their cloud platform. They also highlighted clear opportunities to drive even greater value — raising the bar on financial statement presentation, strengthening Group Audit collaboration, ensuring mission-critical Lockdown for regulator-ready file closure, and delivering seamless roll-forward that preserves momentum period to period. As we head into Q4 and plan for FY27, our opportunity is clear: invest in our highest-value workflows to deliver true customer delight. 

 

*	On AI, we’re translating rapid progress into clear customer value. As we continue to deliver across our three AI archetypes — agentic AiDA, assurance & financial reporting workflow automation, and ecosystem workflow automation — we’re grounding our AI work in how customers experience value in practice. That value shows up in five ways:

 

	1.	Enhancing professional judgment and efficiency through an engagement-aware assistant embedded in daily work;

 

	2.	Automating tedious, highly manual, repetitive work inside complex engagements;

 

	3.	Giving enterprise firms the combined power of AI and methodological control wi...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-16 23:43 | Inside the Studio – Week 20 | Clearing the path forward
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
Toronto got a proper dump of snow this week. You don’t clear a city in one heroic push—you keep the lanes open with steady passes. That’s our week: one pass to shape how file‑centric work should flow across the platform (design sprint), another to get the Document Analysis Agent ready for decisions so it can meet CWX with confidence. Our job stays the same: turn uncertainty into proof fast—where customers feel it.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
Design sprint → platform interaction (not agent testing)
We focused on file‑centric interaction patterns across the platform:

*	Where actions live (in‑file triggers, context menus, task‑specific surfaces).

*	How actions are invoked — deliberately comparing buttons vs chat, and biasing to job‑dedicated interfaces when the task is clear.

*	When triggers appear — state/role/template awareness so the platform feels timely, not noisy.

*	New interface concepts that complete whole jobs end‑to‑end (purpose‑built and auditable), independent of any single agent.

Quick refresher: a design sprint <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.gv.com%2Fsprint%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C9447f2473ccc4a6b8c8d08de55590d0e%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C639042038234217455%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=Ei5f8IG46Dof%2FMcwUbg1Fc6ycyXtaVbxH0w1aJACmQY%3D&reserved=0>  is a time‑boxed loop to go from hunch → shared direction (map, sketch, decide, prototype just enough to reason about trade‑offs). This sprint shaped platform UX principles; we did not test agent functionality.
Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
CWX: first pass defined

*	Templates defined in SE, leveraged in Excel.

*	From Excel, users upload documents to Cloud; SE runs against the template, results flow back into Excel, and documents are stored in Cloud for tracea...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-12 13:17 | Week 47 Product Team Update | PBC Strategy, Elevating #IBP, and Q4 Roadmap Planning 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

 

*	PBC: Fixing a Frustrating, High-Leverage Workflow 
	PBC is one of the most manual, error-prone parts of the audit — and a top source of frustration for firms. Most rely on disconnected emails, spreadsheets, and portals that create inefficiency, delays, and downstream audit risk. Today, Caseware supports PBC through Queries — a functionally sound but underinvested capability that now accounts for ~1% of ARR. We’ve aligned on a new dual-track strategy: for large and mid-sized firms, we’ll embed intelligent automation into PBC using Extractly; for enterprise firms, we’ll integrate with existing PBC tools to provide unified visibility inside Caseware. This direction will be finalized as we complete FY27 Planning. 

 

*	#IBP Cadence Upgraded 
	Pillar Five of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice strategy is to make How We Build a competitive advantage. To that end — and in line with best-in-class product organizations like Amazon, Meta, Google, and Stripe — weekly Impact, Blockers, and Priorities (#IBP) reporting is a core operating mechanic in our Product Operating System. It enables every member of our team to be intentional with their time and to surface blockers for fast, decisive resolution. In the spirit of continuous improvement, we are moving #IBP reporting to Fridays, allowing Product Ops to synthesize inputs on Monday and bring blockers to Tuesday PLT ready for action. 

 

*	Q4 Roadmap Priorities 
	Quarterly Roadmap Planning sessions run Tuesday and Thursday to lock prioritization, scope, dependencies, and resourcing for Q4, beginning February 1. Q4 execution is anchored on five priorities: (1) winning busy season through Cloud performance and scalability, (2) accelerating agentic AI delivery, (3) delivering the Baker Tilly 23 with enhancements built to be global by design, and converting (4) Extractly and (5) ecosystem momentum into scaled customer impact. 

 

And now — onto the What, How, and Why of our ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-09 21:37 | Inside the Studio – Week 19 | From customer pain to a shippable Alpha
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
I hope everyone got a real reset over the holidays — whether that meant unlimited family time or getting locked in with movies. For me, this time of year is always reflection + recharge, and I’m coming back into 2026 focused and refreshed. This first week back, we turned the Document Analysis Agent from “theory” into execution: real effort, real resourcing, and a clear path to an Alpha that completes the job-to-be-done — with Excel as the canvas and Cloud/AI doing the heavy lifting underneath.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
Document Analysis Agent — one path now, seamless paths later
From recent customer conversations, the pain is consistent: teams are forced to bounce between tools to get work done — and each hop creates friction, rework, and risk.
Our direction is clear:

*	Start + finish in the surface you choose — and over time, move between Cloud ↔ Excel without losing context, traceability, or momentum.

*	We’ll get there in steps. Milestone #1 is a single, reliable path (start in one place, finish in one place) that we can build on until the “seamless switching” experience is real.

The extraction pain we’re solving (without giving too much away)

A few patterns showed up strongly from roles closest to the work:

*	“I have to know where the answer is before I can extract it.” Today, teams often need prior knowledge of where the data lives inside a document to build templates or configure extraction.

*	Every exception becomes a template rebuild. If the document structure changes (different language, different format, different placement), workflows fracture and people end up making “yet another template” for the same task.

*	Document size shouldn’t change the outcome. Whether it’s a short file or a massive PDF, teams need the system to reliably find what matters, pull it forward, and explain where it came from.

The world we’re building:

*	The agent knows what it’s looking for, not just where it was last time.

*	It can han...
```

From: Mitchell Payne <mitchell.payne@caseware.com>
Sent: Tuesday, December 30, 2025 11:26
To: Madalina Doroftei <madalina.doroftei@caseware.com>
Cc: Ashley Goldsmith <ashley.goldsmith@caseware.com>; Jonathan Millar <jonathan.millar@caseware.com>; Natasha Kobrak <Natasha.Kobrak@caseware.com>
Subject: Re: Caseware - Busy Season Release Policy (Effective Jan 1 – Jun 30 2026) 

Hi Madalina,

Thank you for this, it sounds like this will be quite beneficial for stability.

We just have a few points on the UK side of things we wanted to confirm.

*	
	Does this impact our local solution updates/releases, and if so, to what extent? We very regularly release content updates for our solutions so there's a concern that this will cause an added layer of difficulty getting these out to our users.
*	
	What is the impact on the feature roadmaps? It sounds like the introduction of new platform features will be massively affected. We have several notable features that we are waiting on (such as style sheets in financials) for which we have communicated some rough timelines with customers.

In the meantime, happy new year!

Cheers,

Mitchell Payne
Product Owner - Cloud and Analytics
mitchell.payne@caseware.com <mailto:mitchell.payne@caseware.com> 

Caseware UK Ltd. Trading Address: County Gate 2, Staceys Street, M...

## [TO/CC] 2026-01-05 13:01 | Week 46 Product Team Update | Q4 Roadmap Planning, Baseline Q4 Deliveries, and FY27 Momentum 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

Welcome back! 

 

I hope each of you had a relaxing and fun-filled winter break. As we start the new year, I’m grateful to be part of a team motivated to drive bold outcomes for the communities we serve. 

 

Here’s to Powering Trust in the Global Economy — together. 

 

Happy New Year! 

________________________________________

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

 

*	Q4 Roadmap Planning Begins: Q4 kicks off February 1, with Quarterly Roadmap Planning starting January 9. Please ensure Q4 Jira hygiene is complete by January 9 (mandatory) to enable effective planning.  Jovin, please confirm this has been finalized across the team to Yuning & me by EOD Jan 9.  TY!

 

*	Early View of Q4 Delivery Direction: As part of Q4 planning, we’re aligning around an early, directional view of Q4 deliveries — including foundational Cloud Platform and Smart Engagement improvements, and planned customer-facing releases across North America, EMEA, APAC, and International markets (see below for details). Final scope will be confirmed through Q4 Roadmap Planning.

 

*	Q4 Execution + FY27 Planning in Parallel: While we plan and execute Q4, we are continuing in parallel to mature our FY27 3/1Y Product Strategy and Roadmap, building on the strong work completed in November and December to ensure a smooth transition into FY27. 

 

________________________________________

 

And now — onto the What, How, and Why of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice product motion. 

 

1. What We Build — Early View of Q4 Delivery Direction 

 

This week, we officially kick off Q4 Roadmap Planning. While the final, committed Q4 roadmap will be confirmed over the next few weeks, I wanted to share an early, directional view of what we expect to deliver in Q4 — all subject to final confirmation through our planning process by the end of the month.

 

At the foundation, our Cloud Platform and Smart Engagement (SE) teams are planning meaningful scale and usability improvements — advanc...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-12-22 13:55 | Week 44 Product Team Update | Celebrating Impact, Q4 QRP, and FY27 Next Steps
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

What You Need to Know This Week

 

*	Recognizing Leadership: At last week’s Product All Hands, we celebrated Kyle and Stacie’s 15 years of leadership — highlighting how our values of Domain Depth + Reinvention, Team, and Extreme Ownership directly underpin Caseware’s rare global scale and impact.

 

*	Q4 QRP & Jira Hygiene: Q4 kicks off February 1, with Quarterly Roadmap Planning beginning January 9. Q4 Jira hygiene (including required fields, dates, links, and one-pagers) is mandatory by January 9 to unlock our Q4 operating rhythm.

 

*	FY27 3/1Y Strategy & Roadmap: With preliminary FY27 squad roadmaps and strategic two-pagers complete, and a V.0 Thematic Bottoms-Up FY27 Roadmap shared with Finance on Friday and Commercial this morning, we now move into defining FY27 Input Goals and aligning on the key decisions required to prioritize our FY27 roadmap.

 

*	Extractly Go-Live — Status Green: We remain on track for the January 5 multi-market Extractly Bundle launch — all regions deployed, Microsoft Store approval complete, activation sequencing ready, and the Artifex contract dependency resolved for a January 1 start.

 

*	Next Weekly Update will be Jan 5: With Caseware closed between Christmas and New Year’s, there will be no update next week — see you back here on January 5! 

 

And now — onto the What, How, and Why of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice product motion. 

 

1. What We Build — Celebrating Impact & 15 Years of Leadership 

 

Thank you to everyone who joined our Product All Hands last Thursday, where we reviewed our AI Strategy, heard the latest on our Agentic product initiatives, and celebrated our team's impressive impact YTD in FY27. 

 

 

 

I also shared something we acknowledge far too rarely: around the world, when accounting firms reflect on the technology they depend on every single day to run their business, two names consistently rise above the rest — Microsoft and Caseware. That is the company we keep in the mind...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-12-19 21:37 | Inside the Studio – Week 16 | Finish strong so we can start stronger!
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
Week 16 is the last Inside the Studio for this year. The next update will be Week 19, landing on January 9, so we can keep the week count aligned with the calendar and pick up the story cleanly in 2026.
This week was about finishing strong on the thing that matters most for next year: Document Analysis Agent (what some of you knew as Extractly AI). We got closer to real customer pain, confirmed we’re chasing the right seams, and locked in more conversations so we can come back in January and move fast on the right problems.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
We spent time with a senior accountant who lives in the middle of the audit grind every day — portals, Excel plugins, extraction tools, and in-house dashboards all stitched together by hand. A few things were painfully clear: too many hops between systems, too many versions of the “same” document, and rising pressure to document work so that someone who wasn’t there can still understand what was done and why.
That’s exactly the gap Document Analysis Agent is designed to close. Instead of “download from portal → upload to tool → copy into Excel,” we’re aiming for a single rail: client uploads into a portal, Cloud + AI handle reading and normalization, and auditors review, override, and sign off in Excel with clear citations back to the underlying documents. Leases are still our first seam, but the pattern is built to carry contracts, policies, bank/loan docs, and other document-heavy workflows once we prove it.
We’re holding to a simple commitment for June CwX: have something real in customers’ hands, not just a demo. That means a concrete Document Analysis Agent flow that starts from real client documents and lands in an Excel experience that reviewers can actually use.
Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
The interview reinforced that firms are already juggling a small ecosystem of tools: portals, extraction utilities, Excel add-ins, plus their own dashboards ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-12-15 13:39 | Week 43 Product Team Update | LeaseJava Progress, FY26 Core Delivery, and Extractly Go-Live 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

What You Need to Know This Week

 

*	LeaseJava: Post H1 stability & scalability investments, LeaseJava now reliably supports US mid-sized firms at scale (90+ US firms, 20,000+ leases YTD), with H2 focused on extension to UK mid-sized firms through FRS 102 enablement. FY27 Planning will address the timeline for enablement of enterprise-grade firms.

 

*	FY26 Core Delivery: 19 Input Goals are Green and 3 are Yellow, with clear recovery paths — database capacity work in December (P1-IG1), intentional P2-IG1 trade-offs to deliver Baker Tilly D31, and a pending Caseware Design System investment decision for Firm of the Future UX (P1-IG3).

 

*	Cloud & Analytics: Cloud now represents 23% of ARR, all Cloud products are growing at double-digit rates, migrations are up 184% year-over-year, and we’re consolidating product and revenue data into Microsoft Fabric to power faster, more informed product decisions.

 

*	Extractly Bundle: Status is Green for the January 5 multi-market Extractly bundle Go-Live — all regions deployed, Microsoft Store approval received, and renewals plus activation set to showcase our platform value proposition & cross-sell opportunity, and to extend our document intelligence footprint.

 

*	Baker Tilly D31: We are green on the Baker Tilly December 31 scope of work, but remain laser focused on continuing to execute with rigor & velocity on the broader “Baker Tilly 23” set of deliverables. 

 

And now — onto the What, How, and Why of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice product motion. 

 

1. What We’re Building — LeaseJava 

 

Lease accounting remains one of the most time-consuming and error-prone workflows in assurance. Many firms still rely on spreadsheets to prepare lease calculations and disclosures — a manual, fragmented process that increases audit risk and doesn’t scale. As standards grow more prescriptive, this workflow is becoming a persistent bottleneck for firms and their clients. 

 

LeaseJava is Caseware’s cloud-bas...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-12-12 22:47 | Inside the Studio – Week 15 | The part you can’t see ... yet
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,

Some weeks are about what ships; this one was about what forms underneath — the part of the iceberg most people can’t see yet. Long days, early mornings, late-night messages, and a lot of “does this scale?” conversations.

What’s taking shape under the waterline is the Document Analysis Agent — the Excel-first, Cloud-backed pattern we’ve been talking about — built as a platform capability, not a single lease feature. Leases are the first seam we’ll run through it, but the goal is clear: a reusable set of rails for any document-heavy workflow we care about next.

I’m incredibly proud of the time, the judgment, and the coordination it took to get us here.

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

Document Analysis Agent — not just leases, not just a feature

The work we’d been calling “Excel-first document intelligence” now has a name and a clearer shape: Document Analysis Agent.

This week we tightened and, in a few key places, deliberately expanded scope so it can serve more than one workflow:

*	Reusable by design. The same pattern — template in Cloud, documents in, AI in the middle, answers with citations in Excel — is being built so it can handle leases first, then contracts, policies, bank/loan docs, and beyond.

*	Platform, not point solution. We’re defining the toolbox (prompts, policies, document spine, evidence model) so other teams can plug in new use cases without rebuilding the stack.

*	Auditor-first surface. Excel stays the canvas where teams think, calculate, and sign off; Cloud handles the heavy normalization and traceability underneath.

For engagement teams, that means less time hunting through PDFs and re-keying, more time on judgment — and a path to bring more workflows onto the same rails over time.

Early flows and wireframes on the path to CwX

To get the Document Analysis Agent ready:

*	We’ve drafted rough end-to-end flows and wireframes from “documents land” → “AI + Cloud do the reading and normalization” → “teams w...
```

	Some people who received this message don't often get email from josh.pike@shawgibbs.com. Learn why this is important <https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification>  
	

Hi Jonathan,

 

It was great chatting last week, and I really appreciate your openness to feedback!

 

Having thought about this for a while, I’ve put together a few quick win enhancements of Caseware Cloud Audit, deliberately chosen for their (hopefully) low development friction and high user return, rather than long term roadmap items (this excludes anything relating to PBC). 

For each, I have tried to be specific in the issue, and possible solutions. 

 

At a high level, the suggestions focus on the following areas:

*	Adjusting journal schedules by type, company and profit impact
*	Visibility of adjustments throughout the engagement
*	Bulk settings applied consistently across the file
*	Account assignment workflow
*	UI and data display across key documents

 

Happy to talk through any of these in more detail or provide screenshots / short clips if that would help. Thanks again for being open to improving usability – I think even a handful of these changes would have a noticeable impact.

 

Kind regards,

 

Josh 

 

________________________________

 

1.      Adjustments and journal schedules

	a.	Adjustments tab:

		*	Tabular Excel export: This is a big step forward but still doesn’t consistently split the schedule by adjustment type, include the profit impact, or show which company an adjustment relates to.
		*	Filtering: Cannot currently filter by company.
		*	Print formatting: Listing adjustments in a worksheet format would allow print settings to be applied cleanly.
		*	Prior year identification: Hard to identify prior year adjustments posted in the current year.

	b.	A27:

		*	Does not include specific accounts or account numbers.
		*	Unable to filter by subsidiary.
		*	Text wraps and does not effectively use screen real estate.
		*	No clear application of group materiality 

 

...
