## [TO/CC] 2025-12-08 14:00 | Week 42 Product Team Update | Enterprise Enablement, FY27 Planning, and Document Intelligence 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

Last week, we delivered on a major enterprise milestone, advanced FY27 planning with clarity and discipline, and advanced our AI strategy by focusing on how we help auditors quickly find and understand the information they need inside documents. Thank you for the pace and ownership each of you continue to bring. 

 

What You Need to Know 

 

*	Enterprise-Grade Delivery: Successfully delivered complex, high-stakes cloud-site consolidation for CBIZ + Marcum — a milestone that solidifies CBIZ as our second-largest customer and reinforces our ability to operate at true enterprise scale.

 

*	FY27 Planning: Phase 2 underway — PM Scoping Packs due Dec 12; PLT Strategy Papers due Dec 19. Office hours posted (see below).

 

*	Extractly + Agentic: Advancing Extractly toward document intelligence — closing the gap in how auditors find, interpret, and understand information inside documents, a time-consuming process that remains fully manual today.

 

*	Extractly Bundle: On-track for January 2026; Microsoft Store approval is the final dependency. 

 

1. What We Build — M&A Enablement for Enterprise-Grade Firms 

 

Under Pillar 3 of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice strategy, we’re focused on Propelling U.S. Market Penetration by Enabling Enterprise-Grade Firm Capability. For enterprise firms, that increasingly includes executing mergers and acquisitions without disruption. Last week, we delivered a defining example. 

 

CBIZ, the 8th largest accounting firm in the U.S. and a Caseware customer for over 20 years, recently acquired Marcum, a top 25 U.S. firm. To unify operations, CBIZ significantly expanded its Caseware commitment — placing its trust in our ability to merge systems, eliminate friction, and power a single operational model across the combined firm. 

Discovery began in late 2024, with planning and execution building through 2025. Daily execution meetings ramped in November, culminating in a successful cloud site consolidation over U.S. Th...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-12-05 21:21 | Inside the Studio – Week 13 | Tightening the Excel‑first loop for CWX
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,
Last week we picked the first path to follow: Excel as the canvas, Cloud as the brain, AI in the middle. This week was about getting tighter — expanding what “Excel‑first document intelligence” (Extractly Cloud AI) can be, then contracting down to what we can credibly ship in time for CwX.
I spent hours with methodology and technology SMEs, closing the gap between “how firms really work” and “what AI + Cloud can safely do.” We’re close, not done — and next week is about turning that alignment into a clear, shared plan the whole org can see and build against.
Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
Excel‑first document intelligence (Extractly Cloud AI) — shaping for CwX
We’re moving from “cool idea” to a concrete CwX‑ready experience for our customers:

*	We now have an end‑to‑end user journey mapped, from “client documents land” → “AI and Cloud handle more of the reading, normalization, and traceability” → “auditors work, edit and sign off in Excel/Cloud.”

*	Roles, systems, and touchpoints are called out explicitly so we know who does what, where, and with which constraints.

*	This work is anchored to real dates: EOD Wednesday the 10th for internal alignment, a board presentation on Monday the 15th, and then something customers can experience at CwX.

We’re also lining this up with future ideas (including PBC) so that once we prove the pattern for leases and document‑heavy flows, we’re not starting from scratch every time.
Internal authoring - patterns that scale, not one‑offs

*	Internal authoring continues as expected: real artifacts → reusable patterns → clearer evidence, cleaner handoffs, fewer review loops.

*	We’re now scanning for the next set of features we can support at scale, instead of just our current isolated wins.

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast

Deep alignment with Methodology & Tech

A lot of this week was deep-dive sessions with the people who know the work and the stack best:

*	Methodology SM...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-12-01 13:27 | Week 41 Product Team Update | Sherlock Momentum, Tilting Our Umbrellas, and Our Right to Win in AI 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

 

*	Sherlock Momentum: Major platform enablement, data access, and commercial gains in H1; accelerating scalability, reliability, and data coverage in H2. 

 

*	Tilting Our Umbrellas: When we consistently design — and build — with empathy, we create extraordinary customer and business value through the small, thoughtful choices that elevate every experience. 

 

*	Our Right to Win in AI: Caseware is uniquely positioned to win the future of AI for our customers — powered by workflow depth, adaptive methodology, and firm-grade governance. 

 

*	Q3 Execution On Track: Cloud scalability, Extractly Bundle, FY26 revenue commit, Baker Tilly D31, and Agentic AI acceleration all pacing to plan. 

 

*	Celebrating Excellence: Congrats to Matt Swann, promoted to Senior Product Manager for exceptional global impact. 

 

Four weeks into Q3, we continue to operate with urgency and deliver with discipline — translating strategy into measurable customer impact across our global platform. This week, we spotlight major momentum in Sherlock, sharpen how we build through a powerful design principle from Slack, highlight our right to win in AI, and reinforce our Q3 execution rhythm. 

 

1. What We Build — Sherlock Accelerating 

 

Sherlock continues to scale as our practice intelligence engine — delivering secure, high-quality engagement data and analytics that deepen insight across assurance and financial reporting workflows. In H1, the team strengthened scalability and integration readiness, enabling uninterrupted historical access through Working Papers Historical Engagement Extraction and unlocking cross-platform analytics by allowing Sherlock to consume key SQM quality-management data. We removed long-standing API blockers, enabling onboarding for several large enterprise clients, and partnered with AICPA to bring procedure-response data from OnPoint EBP and PCR directly into Sherlock — a major unlock for North American firms. 

 ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-28 21:46 | Inside the Studio – Week 13 | Think in Excel, prove with evidence
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,

Last week we came back with a map; this week we’re walking the first kilometer that matters. We’re exploring putting AI where auditors already think — in Excel — while letting Caseware Cloud take on more of the heavy lifting in the background. The goal is simple: more time on judgment, less time on grind, and evidence that stands up when it matters.

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel

Excel-first document intelligence

We’re centering our next proof around a pattern that matches how firms actually work: Excel as the canvas, Cloud as the brain, AI in the middle. The first place we’re exploring this is lease workflows, where auditors today spend a lot of time reading messy documents and re-keying details into large templates.

At a high level, firms define the questions that matter for their lease templates, we connect those questions to client documents, and AI proposes structured answers with links back to the underlying text — all surfaced in Excel, where teams already do their side calculations, judgments, and sign-off. You can think of it as: configure once in Cloud, work in Excel, with AI + evidence in between.

Why this matters for customers

*	Time back: Less time hunting through PDFs, selecting and re-typing values; more time on analysis and review.

*	Stronger evidence: Each answer can be tied to concrete passages in the underlying documents, supporting review and inspection.

*	Repeatable patterns: Once this works for leases, the same pattern can extend to other document-heavy workflows (contracts, policies, bank/loan docs, and beyond).

Internal authoring

Internal authoring continues to move in the right direction — we’re turning real artifacts into reusable patterns that show up as clearer evidence, cleaner handoffs, and fewer review loops for customers.

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast

	These are options under evaluation — directional, not yet commitments.

Cloud brain + Excel UI

We’re shaping...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-24 13:01 | Week 40 Product Team Update | Q3 Velocity, FY27 Planning, and Our AI Toolbox
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

Three weeks into Q3, we’re operating with urgency and delivering with velocity — translating our strategy into customer impact at scale. From regional releases and AI-driven experimentation to the final push on Baker Tilly D31, our focus is clear: execution with precision, pace, and purpose. 

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

 

*	Q3 Global Momentum: Across all major regions, we’re closing the calendar year with high-impact product releases that deepen market trust and strengthen our global platform position (see below for Q3 deliveries by market).

 

*	FY27 Planning Sprint #2: This week, we’re kicking off Sprint #2 of FY27 Planning, focused on completing Start/Stop/Continue reviews and Opportunity One-Pagers — the foundation for capturing each squad’s planning inputs, new proposals, and updated roadmaps. Submissions are due December 12. Details and template here →  251117_PM Scoping Pack_vLive.xlsx <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/g/personal/yuning_wang_caseware_com/EQVZY-n9xsFKh3F1CloRdjkB16W91vD26oJJrzJbbMBJLg?e=7TysJ8&isSPOFile=1&&sdata=NGt6aDJFdi9pSjVQYytqeVFuc3ZsUDVMcStYdlZlRFpMM0dDbmFyckxhRT0%3d>  

 

*	Our AI Toolbox: As we evolve in our AI journey, our toolbox must expand to include best-in-breed alternatives — like Figma Make (see below) — tuned to understand our objectives and, increasingly, our context. Let’s lean into this muscle — and continue to evolve our AI toolset through outcome-focused experimentation. 

 

*	Baker Tilly D31: On track, confident, and entering a critical two-week delivery window — a pivotal milestone for both U.S. market impact and global platform readiness. 

 

1. What We Build — Shipping W...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-21 21:13 | Inside the Studio - Week 12 | Back from the woods with a map 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday, 

Some weeks you don’t sprint; you walk deep into the woods and let the noise fall away. That was this week for me. I spoke with firms, read widely, stared at blank pages until the right questions surfaced, and then followed them. 

In that quiet, the shape of the work snapped into focus: the firm of the future isn’t a slogan — it’s a workflow that honors how auditors actually practice. Plan what matters. Analyze with discipline. Review with traceable evidence. Govern with integrity. 

Our job is to make each step faster, safer, and more consistent — and to do it in a way that feels human to the people doing the work. We’ll keep proving it the way Studio always does: not with promises, but with things teams can touch and trust. 

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel 

Disclosure Checklist Agent (DCA) 

*	We’re actively supporting DCA with the rails customers will feel — review‑first, no auto‑commit, rights‑parity, and auditable reasoning/citations — but ongoing DCA product communications will move to its dedicated channels. Inside the Studio will stay focused on platform and workflow bets. 

Internal authoring 

*	We continue to turn days into hours on real artifacts and fold those patterns back into shared structures so customers get clearer evidence, cleaner handoffs, and fewer review loops. Proof over plans remains the rule. 

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast 

Ship fast, protect trust 

*	We turn ideas into things customers can touch because our reputation buys us the right to experiment — responsibly. Every test is review‑first (no auto‑commit), attributable, and fully logged so clients understand what changed and why. We publish the problem, the guardrails, and the success metric up front; if a test proves out, it graduates idea → demo → in‑workflow pilot → platform rail. 

Ground‑up fixes that roll up cleanly 

*	We solve narrow problems at the seam — then snap them to the larger map so nothing fragments. ...
```

```
Cc: Liam Bond <liam.bond@caseware.com>; Jack Holloway <jack.holloway@caseware.com>
Subject: RE: Lead schedules in Academy audit 

Hi Shez,

 

All Academies have to have a financial year end of 31 August – as laid out in the Academy Accounts Direction.  Obviously some academies may have a shortened period (if they are a newly-formed Academy) but they will still have to make their accounts up to the end of the school year at the end of August.
```

 

```
Thanks,

Ash

HI Natasha,

 

I think it is dangerous to assume that all academies fall within the normal academy season.   We do need to investigate if there is a simpler and quicker way to achieve the goal.  Can AI help in this regard?

 

Again.  Without seeing what a spec needs to look like it is difficult for me to comment.
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-21 11:18 | RE: Lead schedules in Academy audit 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Shez and Ricky – Jon and I are working with Ash on resourcing the specifications for this given we will need someone from his team to do it and hope to kick it off in January. May I ask, if we tell customers we commit to having this available for the next Academy season will that be sufficient a response?

 

From: Jonathan Millar <jonathan.millar@caseware.com> 
Sent: 21 November 2025 09:45
To: Shez Hamill <shez.hamill@caseware.com>; Ricky Mahay <ricky.mahay@caseware.com>; Natasha Kobrak <natasha.kobrak@caseware.com>
Cc: Liam Bond <liam.bond@caseware.com>; Jack Holloway <jack.holloway@caseware.com>; Ashley Goldsmith <ashley.goldsmith@caseware.com>
Subject: Re: Lead schedules in Academy audit 

 

I don't want to get into a war of words here. But the simple answer is no.

In desktop all you had to do was give a range of mapping codes and the lead schedules were built automatically.

In cloud you have to specify out each and every code on a spreadsheet so that the content writer has the information what to do with each and every code. When the template was first created, the mappings were also set up in an abbreviated form on the cloud audit template, so there isn't a simple one for one relationship with desktop.

 

If it was that easy, we would have done this a long time ago.

I do have this as a blocker on my weekly IBP report, so it is at the forefront of my concerns.

 

Jonathan Millar ACA BSc.(Hons.)

Senior Product Manager - Audit
Tel: +44 (0)1622 355 234
```

## [FROM] 2025-11-21 09:44 | Re: Lead schedules in Academy audit 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
I don't want to get into a war of words here. But the simple answer is no.

In desktop all you had to do was give a range of mapping codes and the lead schedules were built automatically.

In cloud you have to specify out each and every code on a spreadsheet so that the content writer has the information what to do with each and every code. When the template was first created, the mappings were also set up in an abbreviated form on the cloud audit template, so there isn't a simple one for one relationship with desktop.

If it was that easy, we would have done this a long time ago.

I do have this as a blocker on my weekly IBP report, so it is at the forefront of my concerns.

Jonathan Millar ACA BSc.(Hons.)

Senior Product Manager - Audit
```

```
Hi Jonathan,

 

We can now add WR Partners to the growing list of high-profile clients who are deeply upset that Cloud Academies does not populate automatic lead schedules.

 

I appreciate it is a big undertaking, but please can we commit to getting this work done before the next season? If not, what do you need from us to make it happen? Unfortunately, this is causing wider reputational damage, and it is only a matter of time before other high-profile clients call us up on this – now that we are migrating them to cloud audit due to FRS 102. 

 

I can quantity the total ARR if this helps to escalate the request but including Dains, WR Partners and Haslers we are talking minimum £1m ARR.

 

Kind regards,

 

Ricky Mahay​​​​

Sales Manager, Practice Sector ‑UK
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-17 17:54 | Reminder: FY27 Squad Roadmap input due Nov 21
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi PMs/POs,

Kindly remind that we’re now in week 2 of PM/PO Scoping. Please complete the scoping for your squad by EOD Fri, Nov 21:

*	Access the file:  251107_PM Scoping_vLive.xlsx <https://casewareonline.sharepoint.com/:x:/s/AllProduct-Team/EX1Gmt9xaklHrZoyWzbHq44BXkYKHV2NSN3FjpeVjzJf2A?e=gVSI9o&isSPOFile=1&&sdata=OFFRVGdXWnNiMUh6TlpGOWdEMW5nVnFmc1BTTEJMMzFvRWdFZCt0WFVnUT0%3d> 
*	Identify your squads: Please read the Instruction tab, confirm the squads you’re accountable for and flag any misalignments to Product Ops (@Yuning Wang <mailto:yuning.wang@caseware.com> ).
*	Capture expectations: For each squad, record the current-state FY27 expectation using the short template at the top of each sheet.
*	Use examples: See SE (platform example) and DK (solutions example) tabs for structure.
*	Timebox: Keep it light — ~1 hour per squad.

Please note: This activity is exploratory input-gathering. It’s meant to surface high-level ideas early and ensure everyone has a structured opportunity to contribute. It will not represent the final FY27 roadmap.

If anything is unclear, please do check with your leader or Product Ops (@Yuning Wang <mailto:yuning.wang@caseware.com> ). Thanks for moving this forward — your inputs will shape the broader FY27 planning conversations.

All the best,

Jackie Liu

​​​​

Technical Program Management Intern

	
jackie.liu@caseware.com <mailto:jackie.liu@caseware.com> 

 

```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-17 10:00 | Week 39 Product Team Update | Recognizing Our Wins, XD Coherence, and Agentic Acceleration 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

 

*	Recognizing Our Wins: At Friday’s R&D All Hands, we celebrated the impressive wins we delivered as a team in H1 — progress that’s fueling strong cloud growth, including a 39% year-over-year increase in engagements created on Caseware Cloud.
*	XD Coherence: We’re converging on a unified, role-aware UX architecture across Caseware Cloud — simplifying navigation, strengthening accessibility, and creating seamless flow across the platform. Together, we’re building the foundation for the Caseware platform of the future.
*	Agentic AI: The market conversation has shifted from if to how and when agentic AI will be implemented. Our path is clear — platform intelligence powering high-value, human-in-the-loop automation, beginning with our Disclosure Checklist Agent alpha launching on January 30. 

 

1. What We Build — Recognizing Our Wins 

 

We closed out H1 with our R&D All Hands this past Friday — taking a moment to reflect with pride on the tremendous impact we drove as a team.

 

Across all five of our strategic pillars, we shipped product that’s creating meaningful customer and business impact. These wins are translating into strong growth in cloud usage: 

*	Unique Cloud firms: ↑ 10 % YoY 
*	Unique Cloud users: ↑ 23 % YoY 
*	Engagements created on Cloud: ↑ 39 % YoY 

 

At the same time, we’ve maintained healthy retention — GRR 95% (-1 pt YoY) and NRR 105% (flat). Cloud ARR is up more than 50% year over year and now represents 23% of total ARR (+6 pts YoY). 

 

More firms are choosing Caseware, using it more deeply, and trusting it for their most critical work. 

 

 

2. How We Build — XD Coherence Update 

 

Over the past several months, our UX team has been leading the Coherent UX Project — an initiative to deliver a consistent, scalable, and role-aware user experience across Caseware Cloud. 

 

The goal is simple but transformative: one unified UX architecture that looks, feels, and behaves consistently across ...
```

```
Great read.  Thanks, Mike!  Thanks to the entire team!

 

Repasting for visibility:  “Firm of the Future” | Platform choices we’re locking now

We’re in discovery mode: defining what the Caseware platform must include to win now and over the next 18–24 months. We’re grouping the work into four themes that translate straight into decisions and demos:

*	Trust & Control — clear agent identity, rights‑parity with SE/Cloud, review‑first (no auto‑commit), and audit‑ready logs.

*	Evidence that Stands Up — reasoning and citations in a consistent schema; chain‑of‑custody baked into every artifact.
*	Configurable at Firm Scale — admin configuration by engagement and jurisdiction, policy packs, and least‑privilege tool scopes.
*	Open, Measurable, Ready to Ship — model‑agnostic + Microsoft‑aligned integrations, a shared evaluation harness, and unified observability.

 

 

Andrew Smith

​​​​

Chief Product Officer

	
andrew.smith@caseware.com <mailto:andrew.smith@caseware.com>
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-14 22:42 | Inside the Studio - Week 11 | Pivot to Discovery with Agent in flight
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,

I’m back after a week lost in the woods with my thoughts. That disconnect helps me reset; this time was no different. I love the woods, but I’m glad to be back.

Last week the team agreed to shift near‑term energy from Enable Firm Agentic Workflows into two parallel tracks: (1) our first AI‑native customer agent — the Disclosure Checklist Agent (DCA), owned by Quinn, and (2) platform choices that enable Firm‑Enabled Automation at scale. The Internal Authoring via MCP work continues to outperform expectations.

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
Disclosure Checklist Agent (DCA) | Owner: Quinn
Automates the population of disclosure checklist responses inside Caseware Cloud with reasoning + citations; auditors review, adjust, approve — nothing auto‑commits. U.S. DAS is the first segment; MVP scope, UX direction, and success metrics are locked; early‑stage prototypes are in test; architecture validation is underway. 

“Firm of the Future” | Platform choices we’re locking now
We’re in discovery mode: defining what the Caseware platform must include to win now and over the next 18–24 months. We’re grouping the work into four themes that translate straight into decisions and demos:

*	
	Trust & Control — clear agent identity, rights‑parity with SE/Cloud, review‑first (no auto‑commit), and audit‑ready logs.

*	
	Evidence that Stands Up — reasoning and citations in a consistent schema; chain‑of‑custody baked into every artifact.
*	
	Configurable at Firm Scale — admin configuration by engagement and jurisdiction, policy packs, and least‑privilege tool scopes.
*	
	Open, Measurable, Ready to Ship — model‑agnostic + Microsoft‑aligned integrations, a shared evaluation harness, and unified observability.

We’ll validate each theme internally and with design partners, then lock the platform choices and roll out progressively.

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
Double‑down on Internal Authoring.
Real examples over the last two ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-14 15:36 | Product Strategy Meeting - Decisions & Actions
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi All,

 

Thank you for attending our call today. Here are some decisions and action points off the back of the meeting and the slide deck we covered

 

Decisions

*	Non Corporates to be included in all existing client bundles in conjunction with LLP in January
*	FRS 101 to be charged for separately to the Financials bundle as a specialist template on a per engagement basis
*	LeaseJava to be included in both UK and ROI price books. 
*	Sherlock will only be sold through their APIs in the UK (not reader or builder)
*	Docuflex pricing to be put forward as £75 per engagement (to be then approved by Dhruv)
*	Internal Audit to be parked for the UK until real value has been established in other regions
*	Sunsetting of HAT Desktop to be announced once HAT charity is underway mid 2026
*	Sunsetting of Desktop Audit Academy to be announced after the 2026 update

 

 

Actions

*	Product meeting needed to discuss FRS 101 and how we support clients on the current global app
*	Sherlock crib sheet to be written up with clear messaging and pricing
*	Look into Jason Bradley’s global Assurance app to see whether this can be leveraged for the UK
*	Send proposed Docuflex pricing to other Product leads globally and to Dhruv for approval
*	Cloudbridge messaging to be created highlighting what works for which template and include bulk migration capability
*	Mitchell to send tracker slide deck to Shez

 

 

 

-----Original Appointment-----
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-10 13:04 | Week 38 Product Team Update | Cloud Scalability, FY27 Planning, and AI Workflows
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

This week’s update spotlights three areas of progress — strengthening platform scalability ahead of peak season, aligning globally on FY27 priorities, and advancing our agentic AI development. 

 

What You Need to Know 

 

✅ Platform Scalability: Our new Platform Scalability Mission Team is modeling demand and testing infrastructure resilience ahead of peak season — ensuring we scale confidently as global usage grows. 

 

🧭 FY27 Planning: This week kicks off our first FY27 3/1-year product planning sprint — thank you to Yuning Wang and Jackie Liu for enabling our teams with clear frameworks and tools to drive efficiency & alignment.

 

🤖 AI Workflows: Momentum is accelerating — with MVP scope locked and enablement now underway for our first customer-facing AI agent, the Disclosure Checklist Agent, targeting a January 30 alpha release. 

 

🌍 Customer Impact: Moore Global has successfully launched its Global Basis Cloud App, marking a major milestone in accelerating cloud migration across its global network.  

 

1. What We Build — Cloud Scalability & Platform Readiness 

 

Over the past six weeks, we established a new Platform Scalability Mission Team to ensure Caseware Cloud continues to perform reliably and scale with customer growth.  This cross-functional team has made strong progress laying the foundation for sustained platform performance and capacity planning — spanning forecasting, testing, metrics alignment, and infrastructure readiness.  As firms enter their busiest season, this work is critical to keeping us ahead of demand as usage continues to grow globally. 

 

Progress Highlights 

 

*	Growth Modeling & Planning: Built forecasting models using financial, system logs, and usage data to anticipate seasonal load patterns by region and product.  These models are creating a strong data foundation for proactive capacity planning and long-term scalability management. 

 

*	Infrastructure & Data Readiness: Developed and refined methods t...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-11-07 15:36 | New Cloud Audit E-Course Now Live and new Gettign Started Guide
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Team,

I’m excited to share that our new e-course and Getting Started guide for the Cloud Audit Mercia offering are now live and available to clients through our LMS. The e-course is crafted in an innovative format to enhance ease of use and significantly improve the user experience. Based on feedback from our previous offering, we’ve introduced several key enhancements:

*	
	AI Avatar assistance
*	
	Reduced text input
*	
	Increased engagement through knowledge checks
*	
	Integration of various media formats
*	
	Simulated software experience embedded within the course

These upgrades aim to deliver a superior e-learning experience and provide better support for our clients using Cloud Audit. If you’d like to refresh your Cloud Audit Mercia knowledge, please reach out to your People Leader, who can arrange access for you.

We’re also planning to apply this approach to enhance our Cloud Financials e-learning offering soon and would love to hear any feedback you might have.

Thanks a lot,

Anthony Launchbury
Manager, Professional Services Team
anthony.launchbury@caseware.com <mailto:anthony.launchbury@caseware.com>
```

```
UK Product Update Natasha Kobrak Head of Solutions

These updates are now hosted in SharePoint to make them easier to access and searchable. Links will also be shared in the UK Products Team channel

 

This month we have passed 1 Million Canadian dollars in annual recurring revenue for Caseware Financials (cloud). This is a key validation of the product’s market adoption and the teams’ delivery across Financials, XBRL, Support, and Sales. Congratulations!
 

The next monthly sprint review will be on 18th November

	

Financials 

Product Development Activities

 

Financials Non-Corporate - We have finished the final development pieces on this template and a full QA regression has been completed. Releasing to production 6 November

Financials UK FRS105. The team entered a 2 week regression period towards the end of October after completion of all content and development cards linked to this release. This includes testing the updated XBRL functionality with the addition of the custom tagging tool. ## [TO/CC] 2025-11-03 13:00 | Week 37 Product Team Update | H1 Execution, FY27 Planning, and Agentic Acceleration 
```
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

We’re entering November with momentum and focus — executing with operating rigor, planning with precision, and accelerating our platform intelligence strategy. At the halfway mark of FY26, our results reflect disciplined delivery, deeper cross-functional alignment, and growing proof that our five-pillar strategy is working. The foundation we’ve built — clear input goals, strong operating mechanisms, and unified product craft — is now powering faster learning, smarter prioritization, and greater impact for customers worldwide.

 

What You Need to Know 

 

*	✅ We’re halfway through FY26 — tracking green on the vast majority of Input Goals, powered by the rigor and systems that let us see risk early, act fast, and keep delivery on track.

 

*	🧭 FY27 3/1Y strategy planning kicks off next week — combining top-down clarity on our long-range vision with bottom-up opportunity shaping from every team. Together, we’ll align three-year strategic outcomes with an FY27 roadmap that delivers measurable customer impact and sustainable growth for Caseware.

 

*	🤖 Our first Studio-developed agentic solution is live (internally focused) — Agentic Authoring — and we’ve pivoted to launch the Disclosure Checklist Agent as our first platform agentic release, targeting a January 30 alpha.

 

*	🇩🇪 Extractly launched in Germany — a major milestone delivered with precision and cross-market collaboration, ahead of our scaled global Bundle rollout beginning in January.

 

*	📚 100% of PMs and POs are now trained on the Build for Trust Playbook, and 100% of our Product team has completed goals in Bamboo — building alignment, clarity, and accountability across every squad. 

 

What We Build — H1 Input Goal Progress & Q3 Roadmap Share-out 

 

We’re now halfway through FY26, and the results speak to the power of our under-promise, over-deliver mindset. Across H1, teams have delivered impressive, cross-functional outcomes aligned to our five-pillar strategy — outcomes that re...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-31 19:31 | Inside the Studio — Week 09 | Alpha landed — and the by-product’s a game changer
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday!

No one had us here, but Game 6 is tonight, and the Jays keep proving the “power of friendship <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.sportsnet.ca%2Fmlb%2Fvideo%2Fweve-got-the-power-of-friendship-clement-on-blue-jays-camaraderie%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C0c29d61474b345098ddb08de18b40e40%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638975358874720949%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=jxnr08%2FeET%2B%2B%2FFUvAWua0HnW1OZ1D0EXPNrw6I8uRao%3D&reserved=0> ” is real momentum — it’s the same vibe in Studio. We hit our first milestone this week and it went well: tweaks to make (as expected), and we accounted for them. Internal testing is already underway so we learn faster than our customers will.

Heads up: I’m OOO next week. There won’t be a Week 10 update; I’ll roll Week 10 highlights into the Week 11 edition.

🚨 Milestone Alert: Alpha + “Sawdust” 🚨 
We’re here. A few weeks ago, this was words on a page; today it’s an end-to-end experience people can feel. 
Thank you to @Byron <mailto:Byron.Long@caseware.com> , @Luis <mailto:luis.bravo@caseware.com> , @Peter <mailto:peter.buk@caseware.com> , @Tom <mailto:tom.cassiotis@caseware.com> , @Mikhail <mailto:mikhail.mokhov@caseware.com> , @Amir <mailto:amir.toole@caseware.com> , @Oscar <mailto:oscar.umana@caseware.com>  and @Andrew <mailto:andrew.allen@caseware.com>  for making this possible!

Alpha demo for Enable Firm Agentic Workflows 
We showed an end-to-end Enable Firm Agentic Workflows flow: from a client system trigger, through the LLM, to governed MCP tools, into SE Suggestions for human approval, and back — with auditability and rights parity preserved throughout. It’s the whole motion: agents propose, humans approve and nothing auto-commits.

“Sawdust” (internal Authoring) — signed and already saving time.
This internal build is a by-product of building t...
```

```
From: Ebrahim Goralwalla <ebrahim.goralwalla@caseware.com>
Sent: Wednesday, October 29, 2025 19:39
To: yy_dist_cloud <yy_dist_cloud@caseware.com>
Subject: Deprecation of Sampling via Transactions Page 

Hi team, 
We are writing to inform you that after careful consideration we have decided to retire the Sampling feature via the Transactions page, effective November 30, 2025. 
What does this feature do? 
The Sampling feature in the Transactions page <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdocs.caseware.com%2F2020%2Fwebapps%2F31%2Fen%2FEngagements%2FAccounts-and-Analysis%2FSample-selection.htm%3Fregion%3Dca&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7Cfb7267d9893c4390776908de17941c71%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638974122078417671%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=P9wtHkxS7BL6S7XBVY09VaeMO2q1hvUKM5Sxsmjywmo%3D&reserved=0>  allows users to generate a sample of transactions directly from the Transactions view for testing and analysis purposes. 
Why is this feature deprecated? 
As part of our strategy to simplify workflows and consolidate analytics functionality, all sampling activities will be centralized in the Analytics Hub. This ensures consistency, improved scalability, and better integration with other analytic features. 
What happens until November 30, 2025? 
Customers can continue to use the Sampling feature on the Transactions page until this date. However, we strongly encourage distributors to beg...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-27 12:00 | Week 36 Product Team Update | Elevating Ecosystem, Winning AI for ENT, and BT D31 Update 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Highlights 

🌐 Ecosystem Elevation: Kyle Silverman promoted to Head of Product, Ecosystem — strengthening leadership and execution across end-to-end customer workflows, third-party integrations, and first-party ecosystem experiences. 

🧭 Q3 Roadmap Share-out (Oct 29): Looking forward to our Q3 Quarterly Roadmap Share-out for our Commercial Teams and Distributors this Wednesday, October 29 at 11:15 a.m. EST. 

🤖 Winning in AI for Enterprise Firms: To win with enterprise firms, we must win AI for their core workflows — delivering enterprise-grade, AI-powered capabilities better, faster, and more securely than they could build themselves. Velocity compounds — every cycle of learning, iteration, and delivery multiplies our advantage and accelerates their success. 

🏢 Baker Tilly December 31 Initiatives: On track for the Dec 31 cohort of initiatives for Baker Tilly — enhancements that both unlock BT’s cloud migration and deliver global platform value. 

⚙️ Project Quattro: On track for Oct 31 Alpha review and Nov 26 Beta review. 

 

Team, 

This week, we’re continuing to execute with focus — elevating our Ecosystem strategy through new leadership, aligning around our Q3 Roadmap Share-out on October 29, advancing our AI strategy for enterprise firms, and delivering meaningful value for firms globally through the Baker Tilly December 31 initiatives and Project Quattro. Each of these milestones reflects how we’re building for scale: aligning structure to strategy, accelerating innovation through velocity and partnership, and translating product excellence into customer impact. 

 

1. What We Build — Advancing Our Ecosystem Strategy Together 

 

Under Pillar 4 of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice strategy — Ignite AI-Powered Ecosystem Monetization by Building an Ecosystem-Connected Platform — we’re accelerating how we connect products, partners, and customer innovation around a unified Caseware platform. As the market shifts toward ecosystem-led buying decisio...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-24 21:02 | Inside the Studio — Week 08 | World Series energy: game plan set, time to show value
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday!

Toronto’s buzzing — Blue Jays Game 1 of the World Series is tonight — and that’s the energy we’re channeling: proud, focused, and ready to execute on big stage. This week we lock in dates and get closer to putting real value in people’s hands. 

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
Enable Firm Agentic Workflow
We’ve crossed a milestone: 12/12 core tools are complete for the initial scope. From here we’re hardening, testing, and broadening the footprint — including internal use of the same agentic patterns so we learn faster than our customers do. The through‑line remains the same: agents propose auditable Suggestions, humans approve, and rights/visibility mirror today’s SE controls. Fast cycles on the UI continue; the biggest unlock now is shipping into real hands. Key dates; 

*	
	Alpha [October 31]: Internal-only, narrow, end‑to‑end flow to feel the experience and validate the rails.
*	
	Beta [November 26]: Invite‑only, near‑final UX; feedback drives last‑mile changes.
*	
	GA [December 17]: Production launch for Enable Firm Agentic Workflows.

AI Platform Evolution ("Firm of the Future")
This process is ongoing, and we’ve selected the platform direction we’re moving forward with. With a roadmap through September 2026, the platform gives us everything needed to make AiDA more agentic while enabling additional automations and agents across products. The arc: governed scaffolding, clear guardrails, repeatable launch beats.

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
Tool evaluation — paused this week
We paused formal testing to keep focus on Alpha readiness and downstream QA. We’ll spin it back up next week. Current options list: OpenAI Agent Builder, Langflow and n8n. Other tools we are evaluating as well are, a meeting note taker, PRD tool (agent‑assisted specs, review, and traceability), External MCP (capability connectors), Pendo add-ons (instrumentation to observe value in‑product) and others. 

Run the Plays | Turnin...
```

```
Ricky
```

```
Hi Ricky

I'm afraid this isn't really our area of expertise as it is something that is done at server level. I think Ron Lau may be the person who will be able to give you the more complete response.

What I do know is that there is a big initiative being led by Ron in terms of the file copy retention. My understanding is that is due to come online sometime after December, so it may be worth asking him if that will affect existing files or if it will only work for files created after the date of their release.

Sorry to seemingly pass the buck on this one, but I think you would get a better quality of reply from the team that are working on this right now.
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-21 20:38 | Sumer audit help please
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Both,

 

Sumer audit look after 5 x Caseware Hubs all using Caseware audit desktop and cloud audit. They have moved to Inflo, and I am now trying to agree their audit exit asap as they are making me do a ton of added admin.

 

Basically, I am telling them if they want to keep digital files in Caseware and have the usual read only access via WP document manager then they need to pay for WP and for cloud audit they need at least 1 x CC user and 1 x Audit Mercia to access the files.

 

They don’t want to pay for anything to cover the 7-year period if they can avoid it so I keep telling them use the export to PDF and get it out of Caseware and that’s your 100% free option. She doesn’t really get it – please see below. 

 

Please can you help me to put this to bed by providing a response to the below 3 questions as I’m not sure how much work is involved with the output to PDF. 

 

Many thanks,

 

Ricky

 

 

 

Regarding the first email I’ve attached where you were responding on access to files to meet our regulatory requirements, I do have some concerns around asking teams to download, pdf and then store files namely:

 

1.	This means we would need to download, pdf and store 7 years’ worth of files.  How long does it typically take to download files as this feels practically a very challenging ask of teams?  Also how big are these files typically as we would need to ensure there is sufficient storage to allow this to take place?  This feels like a practical challenge.  
2.	We have previously requested some form of comfort from Caseware that pdf files would be readable e.g. from my own experience of pdf’ing excel files, this often doesn’t work – links are lost, files are cut short.  What comfort will you provide that every file is readable and links appropriately?  Again, this feels like a practical challenge.  
3.	It would be useful to understand what our legacy contracts with Caseware state re ensuring that we retain access to files for the regulatory requir...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-21 15:38 | Re: UK CAB Notes - 15 Oct - Internal
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Thanks for sharing Jack.

Also sharing the sticky note board we put together that represents the vendor landscape in the UK and the priority of various integration use cases. I have it on my list to  put this into something more consumable (don't worry - I took the sticky notes back to Toronto with me and will put it into a spreadsheet so you don't have to try to make sense of the image below).

A major takeaway for me was that the majority of firms were utilizing Inflo (PBC and Data Ingestion) and Datasnipper (Document Extraction and FS Review). This obviously represents a significant risk to our business in that those point solutions act as a landing point into the core assurance practice.

Thanks,

Kyle Silverman

Head of Product, Assurance & Financial Reporting
```

```
Subject: UK CAB Notes - 15 Oct - Internal

 

Hi all,

 

Sharing notes from CAB on client feedback/long term strategies. For those not there, this was a positive session where everyone was engaged. Informal feedback was very positive with firms saying they enjoyed the additional insights and knowledge shared.

 

To be clear – I don’t agree with all the below (eg. Inflo integration) but points were spoken about by numerous firms.

 

Given large group on email – any questions/clarifications please feel free to contact me directly to reduce inbox noise.

 

Thanks,

 

Jack

 

 

 

Notes from CAB:

 

Audit and Caseware Methodology

 

*	Firms nervous about moving from Mercia methodology to Caseware methodology, but can see that if its beneficial they would move
*	SAP use - A lot of firms still using OSPs more than SAPs due to data is...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-21 11:56 | UK CAB Notes - 15 Oct - Internal
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi all,

 

Sharing notes from CAB on client feedback/long term strategies. For those not there, this was a positive session where everyone was engaged. Informal feedback was very positive with firms saying they enjoyed the additional insights and knowledge shared.

 

To be clear – I don’t agree with all the below (eg. Inflo integration) but points were spoken about by numerous firms.

 

Given large group on email – any questions/clarifications please feel free to contact me directly to reduce inbox noise.

 

Thanks,

 

Jack

 

 

 

Notes from CAB:

 

Audit and Caseware Methodology

 

*	Firms nervous about moving from Mercia methodology to Caseware methodology, but can see that if its beneficial they would move
*	SAP use - A lot of firms still using OSPs more than SAPs due to data issues from customers – Firms were generally positive they would use more SAPs if this became easier
*	Interim workflows – Firms want to see parallel work programmes which integrate with final work programmes to allow them to be able to complete preliminary work before the year end.

*	Numerous firms are building their own ‘AIDA’ already giving less use for a Caseware AIDA
*	Document retention and Lockdown process was KEY to numerous firms in the room. Lengthy questions on choosing what was available for cold review and giving firms flexibility on what was retained.
*	Open banking links extremely popular in the market – note these are UK only customers

 

Disclosure Checklists

 

*	AI disclosures – software for disclosure checklist
*	Disclosure checklist driving the first draft – decided this was not useful as too many custom notes are written for auditors
*	AI to drive specific accounting policies through the disclosure checklist
*	Becomes a report at the end of statements

 

Integrations

 

Firms most notable requests for integrations with Caseware was:

 

*	AlphaTax – for Corporation Tax/Financials integration – Almost every firm was using AlphaTax
*	Inflo PBC – the number ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-17 20:30 | Inside the Studio - Week 07 | Fall Momentum
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday!

The season’s shifting—crisper air, sharper focus. Fall is my favourite window for weather and growth: prune what’s not working, double‑down on what is, and show real progress. That’s our energy this week: convert rails into results and make value unmistakable.

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
Enable Firm Agentic Workflows 11/12 tools complete. We’re starting the QA process on the finished tools while we finalize the last one next week. We hit a bit of technical debt that slowed the close‑out. The Suggestion UI is locked; development starts next week. We’ll share the launch plan in next week’s update, with an alpha in your hands shortly after so you can feel the flow end‑to‑end. 

AI Platform Evolution (“Firm of the Future”) has two tracks in motion—(1) technology scaffolding to make AiDA agentic and leverage Agentic Workflows; (2) roadmap to empower all product teams to build agents responsibly. This is an ongoing conversation; I’ll keep you updated as decisions land.

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
OpenAI Agent Builder (evaluation)
We’ve been testing it this past week. Promising, but not ready for org‑wide rollout yet. I have high conviction we’ll see meaningful efficiency gains once we scale it—more to come.

Replit rollout (Product‑wide)
All Product members are being added. We increased seat count; a few of you may have received emails but couldn’t create an account (and some didn’t get emails at all). We’re working to fully resolve access by mid‑next week. Thanks for the patience—Replit continues to compress idea → demo time.

ChatGPT | Projects vs Custom GPTs
We’ve been getting a lot of when to use Projects vs Custom GPTs, so we have made a guide but here is a quick overview <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaseware.atlassian.net%2Fwiki%2Fspaces%2FAI%2Fpages%2F1960280148%2FChatGPT%2B-%2BProjects%2Bvs.%2BCustom%2BGPTs&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C9387f848...
```

## [FROM] 2025-10-16 12:15 | Re: Case #01313664: Disclosure Checklist Not Performing As Client Expects
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Ricky

We had this query through the support team from Moores earlier this week https://caseware.atlassian.net/browse/UKCAS-235 <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaseware.atlassian.net%2Fbrowse%2FUKCAS-235&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7Cb88449ac69cb45233ae408de0ca5440c%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638962101146619918%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=XPOkvqCYSZsmldKPzi4xJpMyJji6GoHeuLp6PVsL4NQ%3D&reserved=0> 

I answered as follows on there

"This seems to be a misunderstanding of the purpose and function of a checklist.
A Disclosure Checklist serves as an aide-memoire - it is designed to help ensure that all necessary disclosures are included in your financial statements.
If the checklist presents a disclosure required for a medium-sized company and you respond “No” a reviewer will then verify that the appropriate disclosure appears in the financial statements.
Basically,  the checklist simply records which disclosures you confirm are or are not included. It remains the user’s responsibility to make sure that all required items are ultimately reflected in the financial statements. 
I hope this helps the user understanding why no warning message flags up. Thanks" Simply put, if you say No to a required disclosure - either justify it, or make the change in the financial statements. A reviewer will also go through, see if there are any No answers and make sure that is corrected in the financial statements. The software doesn't stop you making a mistake it just records your responses. That is the way they have always worked.

Jonathan Millar ACA BSc.(Hons.)

Senior Product Manager - Audit
Tel: +44 (0)1622 355 234

Mob: +44 (0)7932 793167

jonathan.millar@caseware.com <mailto:jonathan.millar@caseware.com>

```

```
🧭 CwX London: Our flagship UK event this week showcases Caseware’s leadership across Cloud Audit, Financials, and our connected ecosystem — with clear, customer-centered messaging driven by the UK Solutions team (Kudos Natasha & team!). 

 

🧩 BT D31: Single-threaded ownership in action — Ron Lau appointed to lead coordinated delivery across Product for Baker Tilly’s December 31 outcomes. 

 

🎯 Mid-Year Alignment: Goal definition and development check-ins underway across Product. All teams are to reach 100% Bamboo goal alignment by October 23. 

 

☕ Product Office Hours: A reminder that 90-minute weekly Office Hours remain open to all team members — book a 15-minute slot through Tess to connect directly. 

 

🤖 Agentic Intelligence: Firm-Enabled Agentic Workflows initiative remains on track for January 21; locking in on May 21 launch for AiDA Platform Intelligence (powered by LangChain, see below). 

 

Team, 

 

This week, we’re showcasing the strength of our global product organization — delivering with clarity in market, deepening our operating discipline, and driving AI innovation with speed and precision. 

 

1. What We Build: CwX London — Showcasing Product Leadership in Market 

 

This week is CwX London 2025 — our flagship UK event where we connect directly with firms and showcase how we’re leading the market. For many, it’s the most visible moment of the year to see Caseware’s progress across cloud assurance, financial reporting, and our connected ecosystem. 

 

In Assurance, we’re delivering a major step forward in Cloud Audit — introducing Groups & Central Planning (Oct 2025) to enable group audits and central planning within a single file, and the Mercia Summer Update embedding Advanced Data Analytics directly into audit procedures. Together, these releases raise the bar for quality, efficiency, and compliance while accelerating cloud adoption across firms.
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-10 21:32 | Inside the Studio - Week 06 | The Platform Takes Shape
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday!

The rails are almost all in place. We’re closing in on 100% tool coverage for Enable Firm Agentic Workflows, finalizing our platform choice for the next phase of agentic solutions in Caseware, and tightening the bridge between GTM and Engineering.

We’re experimenting with a new format this week—fewer walls of text, more clarity, same momentum. Like any good product that comes from Studio, it’s a prototype that’ll keep getting sharper as we go.

It’s the kind of week where the work quietly compounds—and the payoff starts to show. Let’s dive in.

Build the Platform | What customers will soon feel
We’re nearing the finish line for the tooling behind Enable Firm Agentic Workflows and are on track for 100% coverage next week. Following this, we’ll update the SE UI for how Suggestions are handled. Once complete, that will wrap the full end-to-end user experience.
Suggestions designs are approved across Product, Design, and Engineering, with front-end build starting shortly. It’s our first tangible step connecting the SE experience directly to firm-built agent workflows.
The AI Platform Evolution (our “Firm of the Future” initiative) is also nearing sign-off. Once live, it becomes the foundation for delivering agentic capabilities faster, safer, and more consistently across Caseware.

Support the Platform | How we make it real, safe, and fast
Our Replit + OpenAI stack is fully live across Product and already cutting prototype time dramatically. All PMs have access, OpenAI API keys are approved for internal-only use, and OpenAI Agent Builder has been added to the evaluation stack alongside n8n and Langflow.
We’re benchmarking performance and reliability to choose the long-term internal agent platform.
On the governance side, the org-wide Note-Taker and the Agentic PRD tool entered ISO 42001 review to ensure secure, compliant capture and workflows that feed our Agent Builders pipeline. These systems not only help Studio move faster—they set the model for how ...
```

## [FROM] 2025-10-10 09:28 | Re: Lead schedules in Academy audit 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
It was always very difficult to get an agreed specification for lead schedules in Charities/Academies. That was the basic reason why they weren't added to those products at their inception.

When the Charity template was first set up (and then copied into Academy) it didn't have any lead schedules. We have added some since in Charity, but it took a massive solo effort from Liam to be able to do that. We just haven't had any space to now get proper specifications together and more importantly the time to get them into Academy. 

I have it as a permanent blocker on my IBP list to find the time to do them. It is too late for the current Academy season, so once we have managed to get through the Mercia Autumn updates we will endeavour to add them into Academy in the New Year.

Jonathan Millar ACA BSc.(Hons.)
```

**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi all,

Please find the staffing for the Tech Lounge at CwX London ​here <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/g/personal/hannah_griffiths_caseware_com/EXjGVJvQpUZHvL7A6JBuQwkB1btxnB_wRVQoGPf64MXttg?e=3xzev6&&sdata=YTNWQmF1NllNV2RHREYvOWxCdlRKeTU2R0d3UzRRM1hvbU01VFd2QmJGWT0%3d> ​. You have been sent this because you have been allocated a stand. The Tech Lounge this year will also be open at lunch, so the slots are split into 3 (2 at lunch and 1 during the dedicated Tech Lounge hour). 

I have chosen this schedule so that those who have breakout sessions immediately after lunch do not have to rush off and can grab some lunch and go and prepare in the breakout room. For those of you that have a breakout session prior to the lunch hour - please arrange your own cover for the first 5 minutes if you think you will be late in for any reason. 

All demo pods will have a laptop, monitor, keyboard and mouse. They will be set up with Caseware Cloud and login details, as well as access to Extractly. If there is anything else you want on the machine, please let me know in advance. 

Many thanks,

Hannah Griffiths
Regional Director of Marketing
hannah.griffiths@caseware.com <mailto:hannah.griffiths@caseware.com>
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-06 14:01 | Week 33 Product Team Update | Platform Resilience, QRP Alignment, and Agentic Acceleration 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
🛡️ Platform Resilience: Following last week’s brief U.S. platform disruption, we’re completing a customer-facing RCA this week and advancing longer-term actions to strengthen preparedness and scalability. 

🗓️ QRP Alignment: Thank you for the constructive discussions during QRP — we’re aligned on clear priorities that position us to deliver with focus: critical cloud scalability, Extractly bundle enablement, FY26 revenue commitments, the Baker Tilly List of 23, and agentic AI acceleration. 

🏗️ Build for Trust: 80% of Platform PMs trained through the Build for Trust Playbook — building shared habits around clarity, incremental delivery, and de-risking early. 

🎯 Bamboo Goals: 82% of the team has set goals in Bamboo — let’s close the gap and complete mid-year check-ins to stay aligned on outcomes and growth. 

🤖 Project Quattro: Strong momentum toward commercialization of Firm-Enabled Agentic Workflows — 75% of tools complete and pricing alignment on track.

 

________________________________

 

Team, 

This week, we’re entering with clear priorities coming out of QRP — from critical cloud scalability to agentic AI acceleration — and a shared focus on execution. We’ll continue strengthening platform preparedness and scalability, deepening product capability through the Build for Trust Playbook, and aligning around performance and growth through Bamboo goals and mid-year check-ins. We’re also advancing our Agentic AI strategy as Project Quattro moves closer to commercialization.

 

1. Platform Resilience: Strengthening Preparedness and Scalability 

 

As we build the global, AI-powered platform of choice for accounting professionals, last week’s U.S. Production SE Platform outage underscores the importance of Pillar One of our strategy: Winning Durable Platform Advantage by Unlocking Global Platform Intelligence and Scalability. 

 

While the disruption was brief, it nonetheless created real friction for a portion of our customers and reinforced the critical...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-10-03 20:28 | Inside the Studio - Week 05 | New name, same mission: Enable Firm Agentic Workflows
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday!

Demos are humming, designs are tightening, and GTM is gearing up—and I want to say thank you! The time and effort everyone’s put in to get us here shows up in every demo, conversion, document, and interaction. Quick teaser: you’ll see a cleaner name for the same work we’ve been building—one that lands with every team and with customers. Let’s dive in.

Enable Firm Agentic Workflows (MCP/Quattro) 
Over the last few weeks, I heard a lot of smart questions about MCP. I loved the engagement—especially from GTM—but the name created friction. As we roll this out across Caseware, clarity wins. So we’re adopting a clearer label: Enable Firm Agentic Workflows (you’ll also still see Project Quattro). Scope is unchanged—same backbone, same workflows, same goals—just framed in customer first language.

How to think about it

*	
	What it is: A governed backbone that lets firm-built agents work with Caseware via approved tools.

*	
	How it behaves: Client agents read the right engagement context and propose updates as suggestions for human review. Nothing auto-commits. We enforce least-privilege, validation, and a full audit trail. Agents run outside Caseware; every action will be observable.
*	
	Why it helps: Easier for Sales/CS/GTM to position and easier for customers to adopt and understand the value—speed + control: fewer handoffs, faster turnarounds, auditable automation.

What firm agents will be able to do (examples)

*	
	Read engagement context via governed tools (summary, risks, TB, statements, checklists, queries, materiality).

*	
	Auto-suggest checklist and query responses (humans review/accept; never auto-commit).
*	
	Propose engagement updates (e.g., risk tier, notes) as suggestions with rights parity and full auditability.
*	
	Locate evidence through a secure index—no uncontrolled copies.
*	
	Map their clients to Caseware IDs to prevent mismatches.

This Week’s Team Wins
Plan beyond two weeks is aligned. 
Longer team plan locked; GTM has everything n...
```

## [FROM] 2025-09-30 10:07 | Re: CaseWare Development cost
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Getrude

Firstly, an apology for not getting back to you sooner with this.

We have assessed the work to update the centralised sampling forms for both LLP and Charity templates and adding in the new mapping structure as follows:-

8 x Development days @ £200 = £1,600

2 x PO days @ £202 = £404

2 x QA days @ £152 = £304

 

Total = £2,308

The main reason for the delay is the difficulty I have had in determining the correct mapping structure to include in the templates.

With the LLP, this is fairly straightforward as our Financials team are well on the way to finalising their LLP template for release later this year, so we have an established mapping structure we can use. It is largely the Company version but with adaptations for Members' interests etc.

However, please be aware due to the differences to the current Company version which is in the draft LLP template, this is also likely to incur time from your end to amend the template to accomodate that structure.

The biggest problem is knowing what to put into the Charity template. The Financials team only have work commencing on the Charities template scheduled for the first half of next year, so there is no established mapping structure that they have for us as yet.

We do have a mapping structure included in the Mercia Charity template, but this is likely to differ significantly from the final Financials mapping. 

This has an impact on the Synchronised TB feature, where you can ensure that changes made to an engagement built on our Financials apps can link up and synchronise with an Audit engagement. With the HAT apps we have gone down the simple route that the Financials and Audit apps share the same mapping structures.

On most of our Mercia apps the mappings do differ between Audit and Financials and so we use a bridging feature to be able to link those mapping structures together. For obvious reasons that hasn't been done for the Charity apps at the moment, but if we don't use the same mapping in the...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-29 12:19 | W32 Product Team Update | AiDA Acceleration, New Design Org Model, Prioritizing Focus 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
What You Need to Know This Week 

⚡ AiDA Acceleration: Firm-managed knowledge bases GA October 6 — a powerful new capability that makes firm policies & methodology accessible via AiDA directly in the context of each engagement 

🎨 New Design Org Model: Effective immediately, we are shifting to a Studio design model to deliver more consistent end-to-end customer experiences, with Ian Stewart promoted to Director of Design 

🗓️ Prioritizing Focus: Thank you to Platform and U.S. Solutions teams for rallying to deliver for Baker Tilly last week; QRP this week will prioritize critical path initiatives with discipline and urgency 

🚀 AI Commercialization: Project Quattro pacing strong — on track for Jan 21 launch 

 

Team,

 

This week we’re releasing firm-managed knowledge bases in AiDA (GA October 6), advancing how we build through a new Design org model, and rallying around enterprise client needs as we head into Q3 Quarterly Roadmap Planning. We also continue to drive momentum on Project Quattro as we push toward commercialization. 

 

1. AiDA Acceleration: GA Launch of Firm-Managed Knowledge Bases 

 

At our Company All Hands last week, we leaned into the five foundational pillars underpinning our global, AI-powered platform of choice strategy — the second of which is to Accelerate Cloud Adoption by Tier 1–3 Firms Globally by Delivering Best-in-Class, AI-Powered Domain Workflows. A critical input goal within this pillar is P2-IG2: Invest in AI-Powered Firm of the Future Capabilities. This week, we’re delivering a major initiative within P2-IG2: Firm-Managed Knowledge Bases in AiDA. 

 

Today, practitioners waste time searching for firm-specific guidance scattered across multiple systems, unsure if they’ve applied the right policies or interpretations. This significant upgrade solves the problem by bringing firm-managed knowledge directly into the engagement. Firms can upload their own policies, methodology, and templates, and AiDA will surface the right guida...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-26 22:30 | Inside the Studio - Week 04 | Building for now and what’s next + MCP explained
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,

Hope everyone had a great week. We’re building for the future of the firm while solving the work on our customers’ desks today. The plan is big—agentic workflows, a durable platform layer, and clearer paths from prototype to production—but it’s grounded in near-term outcomes our clients feel immediately. This week is about keeping that balance: move fast on MCP and Quattro, expand pilots, and make enablement simple so teams can ship value now and set us up for what’s next. As always: ship proof, not plans.

What is MCP? 
Model Context Protocol (MCP) <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fen.wikipedia.org%2Fwiki%2FModel_Context_Protocol&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C1cf96c7fe2594321b0d708ddfd43dc89%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638945190150409711%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=RtLDdyby7PYuVpwSwndz3aPu3t8A8ubjTS6T5vrLr1A%3D&reserved=0>  is our agent-to-tool backbone—a standard way for AI agents to call tools (discrete capabilities like “fetch docs,” “summarize a workpaper,” “create a checklist item”) and to orchestrate A2A workflows (Agent to Agent automations with clear interfaces and logs). For customers, MCP means faster turnarounds, fewer handoffs, and auditable automation. For us, it means repeatable integrations, lower maintenance, and a path to end-to-end workflows—not just isolated features.

This Week’s Wins
MCP scope and tool definitions locked; co-builds underway
We defined the MCP server structure and began specifying tool capabilities <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaseware.atlassian.net%2Fwiki%2Fx%2F4gHYc&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C1cf96c7fe2594321b0d708ddfd43dc89%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638945190150456807%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjo...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-22 13:01 | Week 31 Product Team Update | AI, Build for Trust Playbook, GTM Enablement 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

What You Need to Know This Week 

*	🚀 AI commercialization: Project Quattro on track — final use case selected this week
*	📘 Build for Trust rollout: Training sessions kick off this week, led by Ricky Ng
*	☁️ Private Cloud: Pilot partnership model now live with intake and provisioning process defined

(Read on for detail) 

 

This week we’re driving pace on AI commercialization, celebrating a key client recovery win, advancing consistency in how we build, and strengthening GTM enablement. We’re also saying goodbye to a valued colleague, Natasha Menon, who has accepted an external opportunity. For the last five years, Natasha has brought to bear a deep commitment to our customers every moment of every day, leading Product Management Operations. We thank her for her dedication and the value she’s driven for our customers. Going forward, Yuning Wang will assume leadership of the Product Management Operations function, with Peter, Susanne, and Ana reporting to Yuning. Congrats, Yuning, on this expanded role. 

 

AI Update – Driving Toward January 21 Launch 

We’ve set a bold goal: launch our first commercialized agentic AI offering by January 21. With just under four months to deliver Project Quattro, this weekly update will serve as our forcing function to keep progress visible and pace high. Mike Shoss will serve as the single-threaded leader for Project Quattro. 

 

Last week, we:

*	Short-listed four high-value use cases, with MCP-as-a-Service and the Automated Disclosure Checklist emerging as frontrunners
*	Began customer validation conversations to assess value and commercialization potential
*	Completed initial feasibility assessments and kicked off GTM alignment 

 

This week, we will:

*	Prioritize and select the final use case, including mapping the path from Studio to Platform and identifying blockers and dependencies
*	Define 30/60/90/120-day milestones, deepening cross-functional alignment across Product, Engineering, Sales, Marketing, and ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-19 21:38 | Inside the Studio - Week 03 | Replit Adopted + Decisions Ahead
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,

Hope everyone has had a good week. We’ve made some great progress that you’ll see below.

We made a decisive move this week: our 4-month initiative (Project Quattro) is now narrowed to a couple of candidates with MCP and the Automated Disclosure Checklist rising to the top. We’ll finalize the path forward early next week as we weigh priorities and trade-offs. In the meantime, MCP continues to move ahead to support the MNP pilot, giving us a durable foundation for triggerable agents that complete work end-to-end. Momentum is up—let’s keep shipping proof, not plans.

This Week’s Wins
MNP MCP V0.1 Shipped:
We shipped MNP the first version of the MCP server. While they haven’t been able to pick it up for integration yet, we’re proud to have delivered on time—and they’re happy with the update.

Narrowed in - MCP vs Automated Disclosure Checklist:
Next week we’ll run a 2-hour discovery, alignment, and scope session to define agent responsibilities, acceptance criteria, and 30/60/90-day user-visible milestones.

Replit adopted into our internal stack:
Beta feedback was overwhelmingly positive; we’re moving to a commercial agreement to make rapid prototyping a first-class path in the Product team.

Internal AI adoption trending up:
Three demos this week, and more shared in the channel <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fteams.microsoft.com%2Fl%2Fchannel%2F19%253Aa9924f677f674d34910da6f78719d756%2540thread.tacv2%2FAI%2520Prototypes%3FgroupId%3Deaea9e6f-c0dd-48d0-9bc2-5fb09f0241ab%26tenantId%3D7b8a41e4-2f76-4eef-87d3-f75dd19c0e56&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C96e9385562f34395168508ddf7bc84c2%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638939111267922973%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=6VmxJ9J5yh6o0gODJ6lSemNurLr5KgzvnlQ7ATgy4oI%3D&reserved=0> —exactly the behavior we want. Demo early, demo often.

ChatGPT enhancemen...
```

```
Hi Sandra

Thanks for your reply and for confirming what’s in my contract. 

Since my agreement is over 10 years old, I just wanted to check whether the 20 days’ sick pay in a rolling 12 months is still the current company policy.

If there have been any updates or if there’s a staff handbook or policy document that sets this out, I’d be grateful if you could share the latest version with me.

Really appreciate your help with this.

Best,
Liam

Hi Liam

Thanks for your email, the only written information that I have on this is what is contained in your Statement of Main Terms and conditions, which I am attaching.  This confirms that we pay up to 20 days sickness in a rolling 12-month period.

Is that what you were looking for? 

I can reiterate what Natasha said about your role not being affected and it is important that you take the time you need to get better.  I will also ensure that Jon meets with you upon your return and discusses ways to support you and ensure the work processes are working efficiently too.

Please let me know if you need any further information or support from me.

Kind regards

Sandra Wood Assoc. CIPD
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-15 13:38 | Week 30 Product Team Update | Major US Milestone, QRP Next Steps, Agentic Execution Velocity 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

Last week marked a major milestone for our U.S. market strategy, with a multi-year, multi-million-dollar commitment from EisnerAmper — validating the direction we’re heading and the value we’re delivering. This week, we’re releasing a key enterprise-grade capability that helps enable wins like this. We’re also advancing Quarterly Roadmap Planning following our first Intake Review with GTM leadership last Friday, and we’ve launched an Agentic AI Mission Team to drive focus on development and monetization of intelligent automation <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.journalofaccountancy.com%2Fissues%2F2025%2Faug%2Faccounting-automations-intelligent-future%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C5d38b9b7e3994b414a3308ddf454bb6a%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638935366989580585%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=hri%2FSL5Ph1hYLv%2Be3GoGNcOdyCVFf8K5ytVxSCuW3YQ%3D&reserved=0>  solutions for our customers.

 

1. Enabling Enterprise-Grade Firm Capability 

 

Pillar 3 of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice strategy focuses on Propelling U.S. Market Penetration by Enabling Enterprise-Grade Firms. This week, we’re taking a major step forward with the release of a new capability that allows enterprise customers globally to customize risks within their Risk Libraries — and gives our U.S. DAS clients the ability to tailor Risk Responses.

 

This unlocks a new level of flexibility and control for enterprise firms:

 

*	Enterprise customers globally can now adapt risk content to reflect firm-specific methodology, while maintaining consistency with our core content.
*	Firms utilizing DAS can shape risk responses to better reflect their specific workflows and engagement standards.

 

The feature was developed by the SE Audit Full Stack squad — huge thanks to Matt, Caleb, Anna, Andrew, Michael, Yogesh, Art, Brod...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-12 20:00 | Inside the Studio - Week 02 | Building Momentum, Raising The Bar
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Happy Friday,

Hope everyone has had a good week. We’ve made some great progress that you’ll see below.

This week we had a check-in with the board, and they challenged us to deliver one monetizable, agentic solution within four months, while keeping at least three more ideas in the hopper. It’s a welcome challenge—and like everything we take on in Studio, I’m confident we’re going to exceed expectations.

This Week’s Wins
Board ask → unpacked & prioritized

*	
	We spent 2+ hours in deep discussion (plus countless hours before and after) balancing impact, effort, value, and risk.
*	
	Brought in @Jason <mailto:jason.bradley@caseware.com> , one of our key in-house industry leaders, to guide the conversation and ensure we’re aligned on the highest-value direction.

MNP Partnership → First use case locked

*	
	Targeting Tuesday to deliver a test environment that enables their prototyping decisions.

AiDA X MCP Tools → Feasibility confirmed

*	
	Ran tests outside the Caseware UI to prove MCP calls are possible. Still caveats, but massive step forward.

Single source of truth → Live

*	
	All work, decisions, and docs now consolidated in Jira & Confluence. This is the canonical home for Studio updates.

Setup friction trending down

*	
	VS Code/Copilot + Codespaces/Playwright flow continues to reduce setup time.
*	
	New tooling under evaluation may cover 95% of user needs.

Hackathon sign-up open!

*	
	Any ideas generated from our workshop will flow into the hackathon track to keep discovery velocity high.

	*	
		Form an team <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fforms.office.com%2FPages%2FDesignPageV2.aspx%3Forigin%3DNeoPortalPage%26subpage%3Ddesign%26id%3D5EGKe3Yv706H0_dd0ZwOVmv3vbAsLp9GqFoj0dTXT9RUN05IM0hLM0w4Q1RaV1FNMEFaQzQ5S0xUWC4u%26analysis%3Dtrue%26topview%3DSurveyResults%26qid%3Dr2e47d7f6f0d040f3b04a3f109be2f9b9%26ridx%3D1&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C9578680a3729408f143d08ddf22e9f8d%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-08 13:29 | Week 29 Product Team Update | Input Goal Rigor, Build for Trust Playbook, AI Strategy Execution 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

This week, I’m covering three areas: how we’re driving rigor and confidence through our Bi-Weekly Input Goal Review, how the Build for Trust Playbook has advanced into a fully operational system in Jira and Confluence, and how we’re accelerating execution against our AI Strategy with new workflows, customer engagement models, and alignment mechanisms.

 

1. Input Goal Review: visibility, rigor, and execution confidence

 

Last week, I spotlighted our Product Operating Rhythm as the heartbeat of the  Caseware Product Operating System <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/ricky_ng_caseware_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7bC189BD78-F8A2-4053-A131-20F530019DC0%7d&file=Caseware+Product+Operating+Framework.pptx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1&ct=1756820426919&wdOrigin=OFFICECOM-WEB.START.EDGEWORTH&cid=70eee49e-9824-4a7c-97a8-7ca5bdd40094&wdPreviousSessionSrc=HarmonyWeb&wdPreviousSession=1986f477-0deb-47b8-b528-7b8c43b59b01&&sdata=Vy9BRjd4Z3hhS21kOGRPT0RTSW9Cd000T0YyWDRQeFJ6RTE3M2NwKzZMST0%3d> . A critical mechanic in that rhythm is our Bi-Weekly Input Goal Review, where we evaluate the Green/Yellow/Red status of every Input Goal across Product.

 

Last Tuesday, we held our review for Fiscal YTD through August 29. I’m pleased to share that the vast majority of goals are on track (green), with just two flagged as recoverable risks. More important than the delivery status, however, is what this represents: we now have the rigor to see where we stand in real time, and the discipline to act when risks emerge. 

A great example: in a prior review, KTLO overage in the Carpathian team was surfaced as a blocker to P2-...
```
