Inside the Studio - Week 01

Happy Friday, 

Big momentum this week—the alignment is real, and the energy is high. We’re making AI a leading force at Caseware by getting useful things into people’s hands fast and explaining the why behind every decision - no gatekeeping. I’m centralizing work, decisions, and roadmaps [See Dashboards & Docs Section] so you can see what we’re building (and not) and jump in.

This Week’s Wins

*	
	MNP × MCP — workshop booked

	*	
		Goal: Learn deeply about their Agentic workflow and the touchpoint between our systems.
	*	
		Success Metric: first workflow runs end-to-end at ≥75% action accuracy.

*	
	 VS Code/Copilot Simplification <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fcaseware%2Fcode%2Fblob%2F05da121b11d6736db56638a93dc0bd1a73e6ab5d%2Fdocs%2Fai%2Fprototyping-in-vscode.md&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C733d08b02c8a4576fcb908ddecb5ed5f%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638926988358502715%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=Sph2pQ0okZQJXZO8LVVP%2B%2Ft6bNE%2FuZ8VL7WWGj%2FZv5I%3D&reserved=0>  — friction down

	*	
		Goal: Enable Browser editing: using Playwright to modify UIs in real time with generated code and simplify Local setup via Codespaces that produces a shareable deploy link, removing local installs. 
	*	
		Success Metric: 3+ non-engineers ship real-code prototypes without Engineering support; setup <15 min.

Experiments 
Active

*	
	MCP Workflow Pilot — Scale & harden for client workflows

	*	
		ETA: End of Sept | Success: 1 client flow demoed at ≥75% action success.

*	
	Studio GPT Template Library (Internal) — Central, vetted GPTs (Sales/Marketing/CVDC)

	*	
		ETA: Mid Sept | Success: 5 GPTs in active use across teams.

*	Agentic AiDA — AiDA can call MCP actions

	*	Success: ≥2 actions end-to-end with guardrails.

Upcoming

*	
	Smart Sampling — Risk-based, data-inform...

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-01 23:39 | Week 28 Product Team Update | Q3 Roadmap Pre-Shape, Product Operating Rhythm, AI Empowerment 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

This week we’re highlighting Q3 product planning with early alignment on roadmap themes, reinforcing how we build through our Product Operating Rhythm, and putting AI into action with Mike’s toolkit and challenge.

 

1. Aligning Roadmap Planning with GTM Enablement 

 

To deliver on our strategy of becoming the global, AI-powered platform of choice for accounting professionals, we must plan with clarity, customer focus, and discipline.

 

On September 12, we’ll kick off Q3 Product Planning with our Quarterly Product Roadmap Pre-Shape Review. Our objective is to review and seek early feedback from commercial leaders on the key strategic themes and customer problems underpinning next quarter’s roadmap—before we commence quarterly prioritization discussions. This session, held six weeks ahead of the quarter start, gives us a sharper, more customer-anchored plan.

 

This will be bookended in mid-to-late October by our Quarterly Product Roadmap Share Out, where we’ll provide broad visibility into the finalized Q3 roadmap—ensuring alignment not just across leaders, but with every frontline team member who brings these plans to life.

 

In parallel, Don will be partnering with Ericka and me to drive a focused mission to strengthen how we arm our GTM teams with a customer-value focused perspective on the roadmap. A special thanks to Julian, who has drafted the initial precedent for our new  Product Release Overview <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/personal/julian_mink_caseware_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BA8EC4035-852F-4BD3-9EEA-3D9B200B6088%7D&file=DE+FY26+Q2+-+Delivery+Highlight+-+Claudia+%E2%80%93+Executive+Summary.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1&wdOrigin=OFFICECOM-WEB.APPGALLERY%2cAPPHOME-WEB.FILEBROWSER.RECENT&wdPreviousSession=46268fd3-76c8-4747-979b-5f169884db33&wdPreviousSessionSrc=AppHomeWeb&ct=1756757859986&xsdata=MDV8MDJ8bGlhbS5ib25kQGNhc2V3YXJlLmNvbXwyM2QwYmQ2NDVjYmU0YWRlMDY3ZjA4ZGRlOWE4Njc4MXw3Yjh...

## [TO/CC] 2025-09-01 13:52 | RE: Liam Bond 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\HR

```
Hi Liam,

 

We had a catch up with Sandra just to double check on everything (copying her in too). You are fine to take the 4 weeks off and this will be paid. Please do not worry about your work and role as this will not change upon your return however of course we will want to make sure that you are getting the support you need to not feel overloaded again. 

 

Jon will book in a return-to-work meeting for the day you are back to check that you are feeling ok and ready. In the meantime, make sure you take this time to reset and do whatever you need to recover with our full support.

 

Take care,

 

Natasha

 

From: Liam Bond <liam.bond@caseware.com> 
Sent: 01 September 2025 09:09
To: Natasha Kobrak <Natasha.Kobrak@caseware.com>
Cc: Jonathan Millar <jonathan.millar@caseware.com>
Subject: Liam Bond 

 

Hi Natasha,

 

I wanted to update you following our last conversation.

 

Unfortunately, my health has taken a downturn over the weekend. Alongside the cracked rib I’m still recovering from, stress and anxiety I’ve also now developed a severe throat and chest infection. 

 

My doctor has advised me to take the next four weeks off work so I can rest properly and make a full recovery.

 

Although I understand that taking time off will be necessary for my recovery, I had never taken long term sick leave in the past 9 years working for CW and I am not particularly clear about the potential impact. 

 

Will it affect my work & role once I return? Will it affect my pay? I’m conscious about the situation and really do not want to get more stressed because of the sick leave I take. 

 

Could you please let me know the answers to my above questions and the process of taking sick leave & return. That way I can confirm how long I would like to take off during this time based on the answers to the above questions and doctors advice. 

 

Thankyou 

 

Liam 

 

 

 

Sent from Outlook for Android <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Faka.ms...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-08-29 20:11 | Inside the Studio - Week 00 | Meet the Studio + Three partner demos
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Inside the Studio - Week 00

Welcome to Inside the Studio, our weekly Friday update. Each week we’ll cover: what the Studio is working on, where we are today, this week’s wins (and loses), and what we’ll focus on next. This is about clear, evidence‑based progress on Product & Artificial Intelligence.

What is the Studio?
The Studio is an applied R&D and prototyping unit. We'll validate and deliver high‑impact AI opportunities (Automation, LLM, Agents) in days or weeks, not months. We ship minimal, testable prototypes, put them in front of customers, and use the signal to decide whether to scale or stop.

Two tracks

*	
	External (customer‑facing): Identify strong use cases, spin up lightweight experiments, demo quickly, collect feedback/usage, and either double down or kill fast.
*	
	Internal (enablers): Equip all teams with AI building blocks—Custom GPTs, automation pipelines, and production‑ready prototyping templates—while removing deployment friction and maintaining compliance guardrails.

Team Members

*	
	Mike Shoss — Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
*	
	Mikhail Mokhov — Software Developer II
*	
	Supported by Amir Toole and Byron Long

Where we are today
Internal

*	
	Published a practical guide for using our internal AI tooling across research, document creation, validation, and both technical and non‑technical prototyping.
*	
	Assessing and hardening the prototyping platform to enable fast, code‑based POCs with minimal deployment overhead.
*	
	Establishing styling and hosting scaffolds so teams can launch consistent, production‑adjacent demos quickly.

External

*	
	Prioritizing the first set of AI use cases we can put in customers’ hands quickly and identifying the right pilot customers.
*	
	MCP prototype is in active early development and resonating with customers (see Studio Wins).

Studio Wins
We met with three partners in the past six days to demo our MCP‑powered <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.anthropic....
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-08-28 16:47 | It’s time—AI as a product muscle [Week 27 Product Team Update - Follow Up]
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Good morning, Team,

Thanks to Andrew for setting the bar and the direction in his “Week 27 Product Team Update” email. 

The time has come: we’re moving from experiments to an everyday product muscle across every team. The goal is simple—shorter cycles, tighter feedback, and shippable learning as the norm.

Why AI, Why Now
The industry is moving fast, and our customers expect more from us—faster. To deliver, we need to make customers more efficient—and that starts with us doing the same internally. My north star is simple: minimize the time from idea to customer value (I call this SVOM*). AI is a practical lever to reduce cycle time, raise quality, and focus our teams on the highest‑signal work.

Who I Am & Why Trust Me
I’m an operator with deep applied‑AI experience: 3 years in Silicon Valley; 10 patents (3 in AI/ML); and hands‑on consulting from Fortune 100 companies to startups.. I build and ship myself—daily. My commitment here is to translate that experience into faster cycles for you, safe data flows, and weekly, visible improvements. Hold me to the numbers.

*	
	Builder who ships: POCs → pilots → outcomes

*	
	3 AI patents; F100 + startup consulting in applied AI
*	
	Clear operating cadence: toolkit → champions → demos → office hours
*	
	We’ll track: time‑to‑prototype, customer‑validated clickthroughs, and automation hours saved

Your toolkit to get started

## [TO/CC] 2025-08-25 11:04 | Week 27 Product Team Update | AUP Momentum, Design System Investment, Product AI Toolkit
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

This week we’re moving fast on Agreed Upon Procedures development through global coordination and AI acceleration, strengthening our foundation with a scalable design system, and aligning on a standard AI toolkit to support faster, smarter work across our Product team.

 

1. Accelerating AUP with AI and Global Collaboration

 

In the last month, we’ve significantly accelerated development of our Agreed-Upon Procedures (AUP) product through global collaboration and smart use of AI. AUPs support firms in delivering engagements where a client and practitioner agree on a targeted set of procedures to be performed and reported on—something customers in the US, Canada, and the Netherlands have been actively requesting.

 

By using AI to generate initial content drafts, authoring the product in multiple languages with jurisdictional logic in a single template, and rapidly iterating with customer feedback, we’ve delivered a global solution that meets localized needs—faster than ever before. This is a strong example of how a centralized, customer-focused approach, powered by the right tools, leads to quality results at speed.

 

Customers will begin testing the new AUP product in early September—well ahead of the original December timeline—giving us room to refine based on real-world feedback before broader rollout.

 

Special thanks to Danielle and Jason for their leadership, and to Bas Sperling, Mariette van Loo, Sarah Coulson, Ana Rojo-Diez, and Cosmin Huiu for outstanding cross-functional execution.

 

2. Investing in a Scalable Design System to Accelerate Product Delivery 

 

Over the last six weeks, we’ve completed a deep dive into our Design function and its connection to Product, working with senior industry design advisor Vadim Tslaf (former Shopify Design Lead). The clearest and most urgent finding: we’ve underinvested in a scalable, governed design system—a foundational gap that directly impacts consistency, quality, and delivery speed across our pl...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-08-18 12:30 | Week 26 Product Team Update | Extractly Market Reach, First Discovery Review, Real-Time AI with Customers 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

This week’s update highlights our plans to expand Extractly and Validate into key European markets, the launch later today of our first Discovery Review within the Continuous Value Delivery Cycle (our Product Playbook), and engaging customers in real time through AI-powered prototyping.

 

1. Extractly & Validate Market Reach

 

We are market-ready with both Extractly and Validate across Australia, the UK, the US, and Canada. While both products can already process documents in any Latin-based language, their current English-only user interfaces limit accessibility.

 

*	Extractly Expansion: By the end of Q3 (Jan ’26), we plan to deliver Dutch, German, and French UI translations—unlocking opportunities in the Netherlands, Germany, Belgium, and France. Early testing with product teams and distributors in these regions will validate and refine product–market fit.
*	Validate Expansion: For Validate, rapid, iterative pilot testing is essential to establishing market presence. Our immediate priority is engaging U.S. and Canadian pilot customers to drive adoption in North America, alongside existing traction in Australia and the UK. In parallel, we are collaborating with our South African distributor to explore early entry into South Africa. Broader European expansion will begin next quarter in close partnership with local Product leaders and regional distributors.
*	Next Horizons: In Q3, we will also begin assessing Danish-language support. While Danish is Latin-based, it includes three additional vowels (Æ, Ø, Å) that require dedicated UI localization. Technical work is already underway.

 

Strategic Outlook: This approach provides a clear path to expand Extractly and Validate from English-speaking markets into continental Europe, while simultaneously deepening our footprint in the U.S. and extending reach into South Africa.

 

2. How We Build: Piloting the Continuous Value Delivery Cycle

 

We’re making steady progress on developing and Beta testing the...
```

Assessment Complete

FY25 Performance Management Cycle

Hi Liam, 	
Jon Millar completed the manager portion of your assessment. You must first complete your self assessment before you can see Jon's portion. 	
Why, you may ask? Well, having employees and managers complete their part of the assessment separately helps each to focus on their own perspective rather than a reaction to the other's perspective. Keeping it double-blind promotes more authentic feedback.

**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

Hi Jo

It has been pointed out to us by one of our users that the last two procedures on A29-1 should really have an N/A option on them.

At the moment your documentation has the following heading

Whereas, procedures 8 and 9 could have an N/A responses where there are no material inconsistencies identified

We have simply gone with the Yes/No option as per your wording for the time-being, but I think that is probably a fair comment.

What do you think?

Jonathan Millar

I find that having proper conclusions in the boxes avoids people defaulting to N/A when they don’t know/don’t understand but Ill leave that up to you 😊

 

Kind regards

  

At BHP we value and respect flexible work arrangements. While I may send emails outside of normal office hours when working flexibly, please do not feel obligated to respond out of your normal working hours.

## [TO/CC] 2025-08-11 13:04 | Week 25 Product Team Update | U.S. Delivery, Completion Journey, GPT-5
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

This week’s update brings encouraging signals across three fronts: we’re delivering at pace against our U.S. market penetration strategy, aligning globally on a more powerful engagement completion journey, and seeing AI expectations reset with the launch of GPT-5. Each is shaping how we show up for our customers—and how we execute at scale in FY26.

________________________________

1. U.S. Delivery: Propel U.S. Market Penetration Strategy is On-Pace
Pillar 3 of our durable 5-Pillar product strategy is focused on Propelling U.S. Market Penetration by Enabling Enterprise-Grade Firm Capability. In FY26, success in this Pillar hinges on delivering against a focused set of input goals—most critically, P3-IG1: Win DAS Early Adopter Firms.

To keep input goal success tightly aligned with delivery success, we’ve zeroed in on one firm as our litmus test for Pillar 3: EisnerAmper (EA)—a U.S. strategic account and early DAS adopter with a high-expectation timeline and fully engaged executive sponsorship. As EA’s Chief Risk Officer, Phillip Austin, shared at CWX:

“Getting Caseware & DAS deployed is the number one strategic IT priority of our firm.”

Last Thursday, we held our monthly EisnerAmper DAS Enablement Check-in, and I’m pleased to share that our team delivered exceptionally well. Over a 90-minute session, Danielle, Kyle, and Ryan walked EA through our FY26 platform and solutions roadmap, fielding in-depth Q&A throughout.

The result? Clear alignment. EA’s Chief Risk Officer closed the session by saying:

“We’re very pleased; we’re on schedule and have no concerns with meeting our delivery timeline.”

Team, we’ve set ourselves an ambitious FY26 roadmap for our DAS early adopter firms—and we’re on track and delivering at pace. This is a cross-functional effort. Thank you to everyone who is driving success for our customers.

________________________________

2. Completion Journey: Aligning on Cleanup, Lockdown, and Reference Copy
Last week, we brought together gl...

## [FROM] 2025-08-11 09:57 | Re: Company size threshold when completing disclosure checklist
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
We only received the final update from Mercia on 31 July, which includes these changes. However, we are in the process of adding these to the disclosure checklist right now for release over the next few weeks.

It might be worth noting that these changes are only applicable for entities with periods commencing on or after 6 April 2025, so the first entities which can use these new criteria are those with a six-month accounting period ending after 6th October 2025.

(Resending with the Confluence link; for those enjoying Civic Holiday, have a great day!)

Team, 

Last week we enabled accelerated onboarding for Validate through a major infrastructure shift, and advanced our org-wide rollout of the Continuous Value Delivery Cycle (the Product Playbook). In AI, we’re keeping a close view on industry advancements (ChatGPT Agent launched) and the competitive landscape (DataSnipper announced a major partnership with Microsoft).

 

1. Validate onboarding accelerates with multitenancy shift 

We’ve made architectural changes to enable multi-tenancy for Validate, delivering key operational and strategic benefits:

 

*	Accelerated onboarding: Setup time per customer has improved from ~1 day to under 30 minutes.
*	Developer leverage: Time savings can now be reinvested in higher-impact work.
*	Scalability and efficiency: Multitenancy supports multiple customers without duplicating infrastructure, improving update rollout and reducing per-customer overhead.
*	Cost optimization: Cuts onboarding, infra, and support costs.

 

This is a critical enabler for scale and customer experience. Deployment is targeted for this week.

 

Huge thanks to Alastair, Kelton, and the entire cross-functional Extractly / Validate team for driving this foundational shift. Your work is unlocking faster time-to-value and setting us up for scalable growth.

 

2. Product Playbook – Progress Update

 

We’ve finalized the Continuous Value Delivery Cycle framework within our Product Playbook as our standard for how we discover, shape, build, and deliver. Core artifacts are now in use:

*	Initiative One-Pager
*	Epic One-Pager
*	Slide Deck <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/ricky_ng_caseware_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B1B3F8AE8-F5AB-4793-BF43-3AFAF5D674CE%7D&file=Rolling+Out+Agile+Standard+-+Rolling+Out+-+Continuous+Value+Delivery+Cycle+(CVDC)+%E2%80%8B.pptx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1&wdOrigin=TEAMS-MAGLEV.p2p_n...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-28 12:42 | Week 23 Product Team Update | Roadmap Clarity, Content Strategy, AiDA Progress 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

Last week marked an important step forward in how we communicate, how we build, and how we scale impact. From our first platform-centric Quarterly Product Roadmap Share-Out with Commercial, to sharpening our content strategy, to aligning on a data-backed view of AiDA traction — we’re building the systems, assets, and feedback loops that unlock consistency, quality, and customer value.

 

1. The Purpose of Roadmap Share-Outs: Understanding, Alignment, and Confidence 
Last Monday we held our first platform-centric Quarterly Product Roadmap Share-Out with commercial leadership ( link here <https://casewareonline.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/AllProduct-Team/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7bcf58cb97-7d10-4d05-9061-1cf093fd011e%7d&action=edit&wdPreviousSession=6557f1e4-73f8-764b-c2b2-2301f1167738&&sdata=K1RaVGdQVWxJendiRUpqNkJ2bCtTSXFOeVE1NHhHcGV6OENyRVJqemxGYz0%3d> ). These sessions will be a core part of how we drive strategic clarity, operational coordination, and commercial momentum across the organization. They exist to empower:

 

*	Understanding: Ground our product narrative in the most important customer problems and market needs — to ensure we stay focused on delivering outcomes rather than merely features 
*	Alignment: Reinforce a shared view of our platform strategy and how we're executing it — so every team can connect their work to the bigger picture 
*	Confidence: Equip customer-facing teams with a forward-looking value story — so they can engage clients and stakeholders with clarity and conviction

 

Our July session was a strong first step: we were anchored in strategy, structured around our product pillars, and brought together cross-...
```

**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Inbox

Hi Jon,

Following on from the file I shared outlining suggestions for product enhancements (including planning balances and Query PBC folders), I wanted to let you know that James Clarkson has also created a cloud audit file. He’s added a number of enhancements and recurring issues that frequently come up in client conversations.

James has annotated the file to explain the changes he’s made. It would be fantastic to see some of these commonly requested client changes incorporated into the default content and with the primary author tools we have improved on.

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-24 12:49 | RE: Group Audit Queries
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi all

 

Just one more point to add to the list below please, relating to analytics tests in the Analytics Hub.

 

I imported an accounts payable – invoices data set to two components in my engagement. At a group level, I have the sampling selection tests available from Jon’s SAMP document. If I select a component it tells me I have no analytic tests attached to my procedures.

 

I assumed it would be because I hadn’t assigned any procedures with linked analytics tests to the component (while logical there was nothing that suggested this was required). So I sent the SAMP document to the components, returned to the Analytics Hub, and on selecting the component I still get told there are no analytic tests attached to my procedures.

 

It appears as though while the document and procedures were sent to the component, it lost the linked analytic tests:

 

Group level:

 

Component level:

 

 

It doesn’t make a difference if I look at the Analytics Hub in the Group or Component view, I get the same message when selecting the component.

Not sure if this is something the team are already aware of but assume it’s not functioned as intended (for me anyway!).

 

Many thanks

 

James

 

James Clarkson​​​​

Professional Services Consultant

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-23 12:43 | Re: Crowes Audit Trial Feedback
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
1. Rows remain fully editable after a sign-off has been put in place. | 
2. Accidentally clicking on a response on the screen removes it, even if the row is signed off. | 
3. The visibility indicator allows users to hide questions without requiring any justification for doing so and leaving no immediately visible indication that this has been done. | 
4. The selection of a response automatically signs off a row. | 
5. An unfavourable response has no consequence for the work required. | 
6. Whilst review tools can highlight a modified response they do not pick up changes to comments or annotations. | 
7. Modification indicators disappeared from view when an annotation was added to one of the rows and the reappeared later. | 

Liam Bond

Product Owner

## [FROM] 2025-07-21 17:03 | Cloud Audit Lead schedules (Academy and Charity)
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Mark

I'm sorry to hear the difficulty that the lack of lead schedules in the Academy template is causing you. As James replied to you last week, this is something that we, unfortunately, can't correct in this current round of updates, but is something we will put on the backlog for improvement. We will hopefully be able to utilise some of the work we carried out on the Charity template to assist with this, but writing specifications for these will take some time to do and fit into our road maps.

Unfortunately, when the Academy template was originally created from the Charity template the Charity template didn't have any lead schedules in it either. These were put into Charity a couple of years ago and I am aware that you have passed back some comments on those, in particular the fixed assets lead schedules.

I would be interested in seeing any adaptations you have made, or recommendations you have, in respect of those Charity schedules. Would it be best for me to reach out directly to Josh to go through those?

Thanks

Jonathan Millar
Audit Product Manager
jonathan.millar@caseware.com <mailto:jonathan.millar@caseware.com> 
 <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fus.content.exclaimer.net%2F%3Furl%3Dhttp%253A%252F%252Fwww.caseware.com%252Fuk%26tenantid%3Dt7Z7_mVuEe-ZGmBFvdVZ2w%26templateid%3D23be95abfc74ef119c356045bdd97a78%26excomponentid%3DLbPa3VWob4GAlSphifpSwGBqt0Xt4AK9gK8flkiwvdM%26excomponenttype%3DImage%26signature%3Dptw6mAYRv6_BdwtWK2sMAuErsi44PVlR7onOfN2-B63LRohcxJ0GcxkWI60GPzKKKnqP-_uodhv3xGuHcnayQoQ2Ev2LTu5oDB_yMRpS27XTkcuvGMuAbiL9UZi7TBt84MPlJKm4MHVukVANi1MSgfAsr49W1MlGcCdbrCyD1YpVX6Z6SQh9ZUGNHTJcNFVsFvxJsCTbfKrQaNBFKjLyUbrBjjdgAOOEaboLu3tWbnotl8nAM8mDmKKrCZlLGtMja34J8udbm0221UyiuEb4gGcMPYv5KyTbbYpGeh92i3hrtb0C5JbID6X6W9VLG1H80DVjXMtxwVarAYnQT3G23g%26v%3D1&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C5c1fe0052aab40e0093008ddc87020b3%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638887106112030086%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB...

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-21 13:56 | Week 22 Product Team Update | Operating System, Culture, Central Planning in Focus 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

 

As we align around our FY26 roadmap, it’s worth zooming out to reflect on how we build — grounded in the Caseware Product Operating System, shaped by our commitment to a strong Product Culture, and focused through strategic initiatives — like Central Planning — that drive customer efficiency and effectiveness. Each reinforces our strategy and our shared ambition to build with clarity, craft, and velocity.

 

1. Caseware Product Operating System: How We Build, Systematized 

To achieve best-in-class results — and make How We Build a true competitive advantage (Pillar 5 of our Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice strategy) — we need a system that unifies our practices across the entire product org. Today, we’re rolling out that system: the Caseware Product Operating System. 

 

This is our organizational O/S — a durable operating model that connects how we prioritize, execute, and grow, through three integrated vectors:

 

*         Vision & Strategy: Driving clarity, prioritization, and cross-functional agility 

*         Execution & Transformation: Powering high-velocity delivery through proven practices and systems 

*         Inspire & Grow Craft: Building a culture of excellence, agency, and accountability 

 

Over the past several months, we’ve been putting the pieces in place:

 

*         Continuous Value Delivery Cycle → our micro model for how each team delivers customer outcomes, enabled through the Product Playbook 

*         Operational Excellence Rhythm → including IBP and our core Product Leadership forums 

*         Execution Velocity Rhythm → now live through bi-weekly Input Goal Reviews, enabling visibility and momentum across initiatives 

 

The Operating System brings it all together — a single, shared foundation for how we build at Caseware. We’ll walk through the full model together at our next Product All Hands on August 12 — and align on how each of us contributes to Making How We Build a Competitive Advantage. 

 

 

2. I...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-18 16:30 | Re: Dains Enhancement Requests
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Sent Items

```
Hi, Ricci. 

Alot of these items seem like small rocks rather than boulders especially in terms of the work required and their impact but I can understand in combination it becomes a larger issue. 

There is no formal escalation process for these requests and alot of those raised here are minor in their priority vs other initiatives for example a shortcut to change the document name vs the issues function revamp request. But of course they add up. 

I will say however when they get logged single request basis items without the context of the potential loss or gain from sales they sit in a low priority or trivial if they are some of these changes because it is a very small improvement they dont get looked at like the example I gave above. Even if they are part of a large set of minor or trivial requests that are combined under one client. 

I would say there should be a process whereby we raise this as a potential loss and the value attached in one ticket, split out linked ticket requests attached to this dealbreaker ticket with the items from Dains that are dealbreakers. Speak to platform and say how much capacity do you have if any to squeeze in some of these requests from this ticket of dealbreaker requests. 

We should ask Matt if there is any capacity to add these smaller things in under this context and if so get a dealbreaker list so that they can do as much of as they from these small changes eg expand whole document manager, retain acc code on creation etc. 

Things on platform side for requests like these are already pretty locked down as the priority is on the larger enhancements to existing features but we can try. 

I also think there should be a bit more sales context on the requests and an escalation process but currently there is not  process in place I know they should be overhauling the Dist project which is what holds all the platform requests. 

Kind regards Liam 

Liam Bond

Product Owner

Liam.Bond@caseware.com <mailto:Liam.Bond@caseware.com> 

...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-14 12:39 | Week 21 Product Team Update | Roadmap Visibility, Input Goal Progress, Vertical AI
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team, 

A lot of great momentum last week: our Platform leaders shared the detailed FY26 Platform Roadmap; we kicked off bi-weekly Input Goal reviews to maintain delivery velocity; and we continued sharpening our strategic lens on vertical AI. 

 

1. Strategy-Grounded FY26 Platform Roadmap Share-out 
We kicked off last week with our R&D All Hands, where Sam and I shared our FY26 Product Plan across the R&D organization. By now, you all know the strategy: to deliver The Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice for Accounting Professionals — underpinned by five core pillars: 

 

*         Win Durable Platform Advantage by unlocking global platform intelligence and scalability 

*         Accelerate Cloud Adoption by Tier 1–3 Firms Globally by delivering best-in-class, AI-powered domain workflows 

*         Propel U.S. Market Penetration by enabling enterprise-grade firm capability 

*         Ignite AI-Powered Ecosystem Monetization by building an ecosystem-connected platform 

*         Make How We Build a Competitive Advantage by systematizing customer obsession into every product decision 

 

Then on Tuesday, our Platform leaders brought this strategy to life by sharing the finalized FY26 Platform Roadmap with our Solutions teams. On July 15, our Solutions leaders will follow with their FY26 roadmap share-outs, which we’ll then present to Commercial leadership on July 21. 

 

Now it’s time to execute. 

 

2. Input Goal Reviews Driving Focus and Velocity 
With the FY26 Product Plan locked, we kicked off our first bi-weekly Input Goal Review (G/Y/R) on July 8 — giving the PLT a clear, aligned view of where we’re pacing well and where we need to dig in. These reviews are already helping us: 

 

*         Surface blockers early and redirect support where needed 

*         Track our momentum toward FY26 outcomes 

*         Build collective clarity and accountability across goal owners 

 

The majority of Input Goals are on track, and several early risks are now b...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-07 12:59 | Week 20 Product Team Update | Validate for the US Market, Playbook Progress, Context Engineering 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,  
 
Three things this week: we’ve accelerated product delivery for Validate, we’re continuing to drive operationalization of our Product Playbook, and we’re sharing strategies in Context Engineering to optimize our AI-powered productivity. 

 

1. Validate US Logic & Trial Balance Reconciliation Beta.   

 

I’m thrilled to share that our Validate US Logic (beta) is live and ready for controlled testing. This enhancement identifies internal consistency checks in US financials without note-number references. Early results are promising—we’re flagging just under 1,000 checks on publicly filed US statements—and we’re seeing improvements across other regions as well. We’ll continue tuning our algorithms throughout the beta period. 

 

Equally exciting, our Trial Balance → Financial Statement Reconciliation (beta) is now available for controlled testing. Because the trial balance underpins auditors’ substantive testing, any misalignment can be costly. Automating this reconciliation will prevent manual oversights and safeguard audit quality. 

 

Thank you to Alastair, Kelton, and the entire Extractly / Validate team for your leadership and focus in delivering these critical capabilities ahead of schedule. 

 

2. Progressing How We Build with our Product Playbook 

 

Over the last two weeks, we’ve made significant strides in operationalizing the Product Playbook:

 

*         Confluence as Home: Playbook and Continuous Value Delivery docs are now live in Confluence (link <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaseware.atlassian.net%2Fwiki%2Fspaces%2FPM%2Fpages%2F1725005844%2FCaseware%2Bs%2BPM%2BPlaybook&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7Cfc8606eefc084f12dac208ddbd4da4d4%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638874863397105660%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=2iGoJETrE%2BULL%2B8LCAWFhuAI%2BvldiuXRlZEU22OUt%2Bs%3D&reserve...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-07-04 15:20 | Re: Materiality Sections-level guidance content Issue and Next Steps
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Sent Items

```
Hi Harrison,

We are not using these guidance sections for primary author guidance, so we are not affected by this issue on our standard templates. 

However, we have them enabled for firm users to add their own guidance if needed, as well in their firm templates. 

It will only impact firm authors who have added custom guidance in these sections. And the following scenarios they will be affected 

Scenario 1 - Guidance Added Prior to 2025-05 and materiality document in the template has not been opened after the 2025-05 update. - Will be fixed automatically on July 7th users must not open the template until the 8th if they wish to make changes or the information will be lost and need to be re-added.

Scenario 2 - Changes made to template after 2025-05 release the guidance section was entirely removed  - fix on 7th will prevent this from occurring however data would have been lost in these guidance sections and will need to be re-entered into firm template. (restoration under investigation but seems unlikely, if they could they would have already) 

Scenario 3 - Guidance Added After 2025-05 after this july 7th deployment or  users have not opened the materiality form after 2025-05 in the template will remain intact and unchanged after this july 7th deployment. 

Best regards,
Liam

Liam Bond

Product Owner

## [TO/CC] 2025-06-30 12:30 | Week 19 Product Team Update | Launching Enterprise Authoring, FY26 Bamboo Goals, AI Experimentation 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

Three things this week: a showcase launch that accelerates our unlock of cloud adoption; aligning on the timeline for finalizing FY26 Personal Performance Goals in Bamboo; and continuing to drive our AI experimentation mindset.

________________________________

1. Enterprise Authoring: Enabling Accelerated Cloud Adoption for DAS Stakeholder Firms & Global Networks

Last week, Sam and I reviewed our U.S. DAS Cloud Acceleration Strategy at the Caseware Monthly Operating Review. That strategy pivots on unblocking a core set of cloud adoption barriers — and enterprise-grade authoring sits squarely at the top of the list for every DAS stakeholder firm.

I’m excited to share that we’ve now rolled out Enterprise Authoring globally — live in EU & APAC, in the U.S. as of last week, and launching in Canada this week.

This unlock enables:

• Enterprise firms to tailor workflows, risk models, and methodology in Caseware Cloud
• Global networks to apply a core methodology once, then empower regional customization — all within the platform

It’s a foundational capability driving confident cloud migration, full methodology control, and scalable testing of firm-specific content.

Huge thanks to the full cross-functional team behind this showcase release:
Srihitha Maryada, Matt Swann, Kyle Silverman, Anna Levitskaia, Andrew Gvozdik, Yehor Kravchenko, Gabe Gutierrez, Frank Duffy, Rizadh Mohamed Nizam, Luis Bravo Anato, Caleb Guthrie, Brody Chen, Art Leitch, Kelvin Tan, Ed Tan, Tom Cassiotis, Byron Long, Andy Gibb, Jebby Joseph, Andre Sunkel, Jai Joshi, Ivy Li, David Yiu, Jack Li, Christopher Leung, Ionut Vizitiu, Anthony Launchbury, and Sarah Coulson.

________________________________

2. FY26 Personal Performance Goals: Updated Timeline

Last Tuesday, Natasha Menon shared an important update in our All Product Teams channel regarding FY26 Personal Performance goal setting in Bamboo.

To ensure our goals reflect the FY26 Product Plan (finalized last week and being shared at ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-06-24 17:03 | Important: Product Team Timeline for FY26 Personal Performance Goals in Bamboo (cross-post from MS Teams)
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
This email is identical to what I just posted in this Team, General | All Product-Team | Microsoft Teams <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fteams.microsoft.com%2Fl%2Fchannel%2F19%253Acc763633347b476aa39343c2cdc849bf%2540thread.tacv2%2FGeneral%3FgroupId%3Deaea9e6f-c0dd-48d0-9bc2-5fb09f0241ab%26tenantId%3D7b8a41e4-2f76-4eef-87d3-f75dd19c0e56&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C13fa36d5b21e40dee6a208ddb338a174%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638863778080270300%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=oncJmU3ds7Xe9nhwHNUO1dKB1f6zO0MpX%2B%2FUQBViBWo%3D&reserved=0>  . I'm also sending this email to ensure everyone sees the message here or in Teams. 
Team, important update on FY26 Personal Performance goal setting (in Bamboo), below. 
To enable FY26 performance goals to reflect the FY26 product plan which is being reviewed in the Monthly Operating Review this week, Andrew and Kate have synced on the following timeline for FY26 Personal Performance goals (in Bamboo). You may have seen in the PeopleOps goal setting training a deadline of June 30 to put all goals in Bamboo. This is not the deadline for Product team members. Our timeline, which will allow us to reflect our FY26 Product Plan into FY26 Goals, is: 

*	June 30: Andrew enters Personal Performance goals
*	July 11: Andrew's leaders (the Product Leadership team) enter Personal Performance goals
*	July 18:  All people managers enter Personal Performance goals
*	July 25:  All Product team members have Personal Performance goals recorded in Bamboo

Result: While you are encouraged to think about potential goals in advance and discuss with your leader, there is no need for you to input goals in Bamboo until your leader reaches out with more guidance on higher-level goals. 
Please reach out to your leader or here if you have questions. Thanks team!
FYI @Andrew Smith <mailto:andre...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-06-23 12:37 | Week 18 Product Team Update | Launching PCAOB, Accelerating AI, Investing in our Playbook 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

Three things this week: a launch that shows how we respond with urgency and quality, key role shifts that double down on AI and customer value, and the next chapter in scaling how we build.

1. PCAOB for OnPoint Audit: Strategic Speed, Real Customer Need

Later this week (targeting Thursday), we will release PCAOB for OnPoint Audit — a defining example of how we respond to urgent customer needs with clarity and speed.

Why now? Accelerated deprecation of the RSM Orb methodology left a group of firms in the US without a workable transition plan. These firms had built deep workflows around the Orb methodology, and the lack of a timely replacement posed real operational and compliance risk.

What makes this release stand out is how we delivered:

*	We sourced our own content and defined a high quality, high efficiency methodology tailored to PCAOB engagements.
*	Many sections will serve as prototypes for a new centralized methodology model that will scale across future assurance offerings.
*	Three firms are already in various stages of testing and will begin using the product in live engagements in short order.

Important note: This product will not have a strong standalone marketing presence. First, to avoid confusion with the PCAOB-aligned DAS product coming later this year. Second, due to the potential need to rename the product if U.S. legislation dissolves the PCAOB and transfers responsibilities to the SEC.

This release is a case study in urgency met with excellence.

Huge thanks to: Danielle Cheek, Jason Bradley, Ryan Blackburn, Khalil Challenger, Kenny Koo, Cosmin Huiu, Serhii Kuzin, Antoniu Gribincea, Bianca Bujoreanu, Mandi Mikulecky, Chintan Shah, Mehak Kaur, and Oksana Bielova. You’ve delivered something exceptional.

________________________________

2. Evolving Roles to Drive AI Innovation and Accelerate Customer Time to Value

As Danielle shared in our All Product Teams channel on Thursday, we’re evolving roles to power AI innovation and accelera...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-06-20 15:43 | Strategy Meeting Notes and Actions
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Docuflex

 

Notes from discussion:

*	Call on Friday with SE team to discuss how to strip it for use within UK market
*	Create a very basic offering so as not to put core compliance offerings at risk
*	Potentially offer on a per engagement basis
*	Include Lead Sheets?
*	2 level folder structure limitation may be a risk

 

Actions:

*	Natasha and Mitchell to speak to CWI on how to strip back Docuflex for UK
*	Discuss with Sales potential use cases and show to customers
*	Set up GTM calls to understand pricing, proposition, support and collateral needed

 

Trackers

 

Notes from discussion

*	Could we have a free and paid for version?
*	We should look to have a single global offering as Netherlands and Germany have similar needs
*	Netherlands have expanded on what UK built to include more in depth filtering. They charge for it at 1000 euros/ firm

 

Actions

*	Don to set up a cross regional team to kick start conversation around a unified approach.

 

Financials

 

Notes from discussion

*	Proposal put forward for a 2-stream approach to Financials with upcoming FRS 101 and IFRS being built into the EY app as per EY’s preference to be managed by the international team. This would allow Beth’s team to focus on LLP next (specs are nearly ready for that) followed by Charities & Academies. There will then need to be a decision as to whether we rebuild FRS101 and IFRS separately for the UK market. If so, then that will likely be started second half 2026. This will require Account Managers to not push the FRS 101 and IFRS offerings just yet.
*	Discussion around moving straight to 26 taxonomy and bypass 25 – since this discussion it has come to light that HMRC will not be accepting the 26 taxonomy in time and therefore we will still need to work on 25.
*	Discussion around timing for Academy. We would like to review whether we can get Academy on the cloud in time for next year’s buying season. To do this we would need to start work on the specs in September with a view ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-06-16 12:17 | Week 17 Product Team Update | AI Prototyping, Global Prioritization, Input Goals
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

Three things this week: real momentum in how we’re using AI to prototype and learn faster, a foundational shift in how we prioritize as an R&D org, and a closer look at the Input Goal framework that will shape how we deliver on our FY26 roadmap.

________________________________

1. AI Prototyping: Real Progress, Real Possibility

Late last week, we saw some outstanding prototypes that demonstrate how AI can meaningfully unlock our experimentation velocity as a Product team (with low lift onboarding to our new Cursor AI prototyping tooling). This is exactly the kind of builder culture we want to scale across our team.

*	Srihitha Maryada built a prototype in Cursor to investigate enhancing the flexibility of template management.
*	Samuel Ocholi developed a Cursor-based prototype to ideate on the enablement of in-product feedback.
*	Alexa Tang, Ricky Ng, and Ian Stewart successfully connected Figma to Cursor through MCP — a bridge from design to prototype that accelerates iteration and reduces handoff lag.

Each of these efforts empowered our team to iterate in real-time with real screen flows. Thank you for pushing boundaries and showing what building within an AI-enabled paradigm looks like.  Excited for our progress this week!

________________________________

2. The Shift to Global Prioritization

As shared in our Product Team All Hands last week, we’re making an important shift in how we prioritize and deliver as an R&D org.

Until now, most prioritization has happened within individual domains — like DA, Cloud Platform, SE, or regional solutions — often in isolation. That made sense in the context of how our teams were structured, and much of that work has delivered meaningful progress.

But as we’ve scaled, that model has shown its limits. Prioritization has sometimes been opaque across teams, with dependencies unclear and trade-offs happening in silos.

Now, we’re moving to transparent, globally aligned prioritization — centered around a single set of...
```

Note: This is a scheduled send. No need to review until your regular working hours. 

Hi team! 
Huge thanks for completing #IBP last week, attending office hours and reviewing the comms! Much appreciated as #IBP is designed to help us prioritize and stay aligned. 
Couple of reminders for this coming week:

Weekly IBP - All | All Product-Team | Microsoft Teams <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fteams.microsoft.com%2Fl%2Fchannel%2F19%253Ac6eea3202f1d4d1f86ce5b35a2b7bb54%2540thread.tacv2%2FWeekly%2520IBP%2520-%2520All%3FgroupId%3Deaea9e6f-c0dd-48d0-9bc2-5fb09f0241ab%26tenantId%3D7b8a41e4-2f76-4eef-87d3-f75dd19c0e56&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7C644e12409fc24c1df5dc08ddacabce56%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638856576113890648%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=U2HTecUpvWYvILsGmbRabWHiNDd0xwIyvwtAlHfUkmA%3D&reserved=0>  Tip: Set it to Show in Favourites, for convenience. 
2) Complete #IBP by using the Weekly IBP Form link at the top of the Weekly IBP - All channel (see screenshot), instead of posting directly into the channel. If you don't use the form, your submission doesn't get picked up in the overall list for easy scanning. Once you click Submit, your form input will show up here. 

3) Don't forget to add your team at the top of the form. If unsure which team to select, ask your leader. 
4) If you were away the previous week, you can just mention that in the Impact part, without details, and move to the other parts of the form. 
5) Feedback on the form or process? You can add it at this link IBP Process Feedback | All Product-Team | Microsoft Teams <https...

## [TO/CC] 2025-06-05 12:37 | PLEASE READ: Product Team Update: #IBP - Impact, Blockers, Priorities - go live for All Product week of June 9 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
All, 

In Andrew's Week 5 update (March 24), he mentioned a concept called #IBP, as follows:

"Evolving How We Work: #IBP Updates

One way we move faster: better visibility.

We’ve been piloting #IBP Updates — weekly share-outs of Impact, Blockers, and Priorities — across my extended leadership team. It’s a simple, effective way to surface dependencies and focus on what matters.

This is a best-in-class practice I’ve brought with me from Intuit, and it’s common across top product orgs to improve transparency, prioritization, and alignment."  

Following his note, we expanded #IBP to a wider cohort in the Product team, tested it out and received feedback. We are now ready to expand #IBP Updates to the rest of the Product team - anyone who reports into Andrew Smith's organization. Here are more details on how it works, and how we will iterate. 

What is #IBP and why do we do this? 

#IBP is a short Microsoft Teams post each person does at the beginning of their week.  It provides a structured yet lightweight way for team members to surface key priorities, progress, blockers, and impact - leading to the benefits Andrew described above. This is fundamental to how we manage as a team. 

How does it work? Where do I post? 

Updates are posted each week using a simple form that gets posted on MS Teams. We are requesting your first updates on Monday, June 9 (Tuesday, June 10 for those with a holiday on the 9th) if you have not already been posting. The process should take no longer than 30 minutes a week (very possibly less as we build up the habit) and we are here to help people get started.  Please see the document below for more details and examples. 

For those who have already been posting, we are rolling out a form and a new channel - Weekly IBP - All | All Product-Team | Microsoft Teams <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fteams.microsoft.com%2Fl%2Fchannel%2F19%253Ac6eea3202f1d4d1f86ce5b35a2b7bb54%2540thread.tacv2%2FWeekly%2520IBP%2520-%...
```

## [FROM] 2025-06-02 16:15 | Re: Annoying bug that is causing us to lose work
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi Tom

Yes, we are aware of this and have created a fix for it. Unfortunately, we were unable to action it in the existing forms, as the way those forms were originally built meant enacting the fix would have wiped all information on all engagements created using those existing forms(!).

We have been going through a process of centralising all of our builder forms, including the B30 and B31, so that there is one store where we only need to make the changes to a document in one place and this can then be replicated across the different templates. We released the first stage of updating our forms in the Company Audit in May and we are aiming to release the second stage of that during this month. This will include B30 and B31 with this fix in.

Engagement files will only get use of these files once they are rolled forward.

Regarding the platform update to the Risks console, I would like to get your views on whether or not this would remove the need for the B31 entirely in any case. Can we set up some time for me to demonstrate the changes that we are able to implement?

Thanks 

Jonathan Millar ACA BSc.(Hons.)

Senior Product Manager - Audit
Tel: +44 (0)1622 355 234

Mob: +44 (0)7932 793167

jonathan.millar@caseware.com <mailto:jonathan.millar@caseware.com>

Subject: Annoying bug that is causing us to lose work 
 

Hi Jonathan

 

I hope that you are aware of this already, because Ben raised the issue in August 2024 (#01105863) but it has happened again to Ellie today.

 

Changing the name of a risk in B30-1, which can be as trivial as fixing a typo, causes all information relating to the risk in B30, B31 etc to be deleted. This cannot be operating as intended. It’s really frustrating for the team to have to duplicate their work on these forms.

 

Is there a fix in the roadmap for this specific issue, or are we waiting for the wider change to the risk recording that you mentioned at Accountex (can you please remind me when this is?)

 

Thanks

 

Tom

## [TO/CC] 2025-06-02 12:47 | Week 15 Product Team Update | Values. Execution. Acceleration. 
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

This week, we’re focusing on what enables outsized customer benefit: a values-driven culture, shared operating discipline, and targeted support where it matters most. From how we show up to how we execute, we’re building the muscle for excellence at scale.

Let’s build what only we can — and keep raising the bar, together.

________________________________

🌟 Living Our Values — Team First & Extreme Ownership

At Caseware, we seek to make How We Build a source of competitive differentiation — and that starts with our values. As outlined in our Product Playbook, these values shape how we operate every day, across every part of the product lifecycle.

Over the past several months, Louis Paul Tong has exemplified two values in particular:

*	Team First – We operate as one team. We win together and support each other.
*	Extreme Ownership – We take full accountability — for our time, decisions, and results.

Louis joined Caseware as an ESG specialist and quickly became a driving force behind our ESG product. He stepped into increasing Product Owner responsibilities, brought structure to a highly complex regulatory space, and collaborated seamlessly across teams.

Even as we adjusted focus in response to external regulatory changes, Louis continued to lead with full energy, dedication, and clarity — ensuring the work stayed strong through to the end.

Louis — thank you for showing what it means to lead with #Team First and #Extreme Ownership. You’ve shown us what it looks like to live our values when it matters most.

________________________________

🛠 Operational Mechanisms — From Principles to Practice

Two weeks ago, we began rolling out three core operating mechanisms from the Product Playbook — turning our cultural principles into repeatable, scalable practices. That work is now in motion:

✅ Discovery Reviews – Pilots underway
Thank you Louise Bonnamy for leading the charge. Initial sessions are underway, including early efforts to sharpen problem framing ...
```

Subject: Re: Academy pack

 

 

Hello All

We are currently awaiting the Spring update from Mercia in respect of the Academy manual.

I am expecting them either this week or the next.

From my discussions with Mercia this will not include too much in respect of changes arising out of the AAD, however they are carrying out what they call a "modernisation" exercise, which may result in changing of wording throughout the manual. Until I see the full extent of that it is quite difficult to give an exact date for the release.

 

However, we always target a release by then end of June/ first week in July, however, I can only give you that date as rough estimate which may need revision.

You can be assured that this will be our main focus over this next month, as we are aware of the need to process planning for the August year ends. 

 

With kind regards

 

Jonathan Millar ACA BSc.(Hons.)

## [TO/CC] 2025-05-26 13:29 | Week 14 Product Team Update | CWX. US. AI Leverage.
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,
What a week last week! CWX brought together the profession’s most respected leaders to collaborate on the future of the Assurance industry. Thank you for leading!

Thank you to everyone across Product, Engineering, and GTM who made it a success. We showed up as one team — with inspiring domain expertise and equally impressive clarity of direction. The feedback we received reflected that. The trust we earned reflected that. And the opportunity in front of us is stronger because of it.

________________________________

🌐 1. CWX: What We Heard

Across detailed roadmap reviews at the Customer Advisory Board, Tech Lounge demos, one-to-ones, networking, and firm leadership meetings — the conversations were deep, honest, and forward-looking. A few clear themes stood out:

*	Accelerating Urgency: While firms are at different stages of their cloud migration journey, all express increasing urgency — particularly in the context of the pace of change in AI.
*	Confidence Over Completeness: Firms are willing to compromise on timing of feature delivery if they trust the roadmap, transparency, and follow-through.
*	Lack of Awareness of In-Product Capabilities: Key features already available in the platform are often missed by customers, highlighting a need for better launch communication and ongoing customer education.
*	Immense Interest in Extractly / Validate: With growing frustration over Datasnipper’s pricing, firms are keen to learn more about Extractly and Validate as more affordable, agile alternatives.
*	Workflow Is the Battleground: In the context of their methodology, firms prioritize efficient, end-to-end workflows — including financial reporting — always in service of quality.

Competitive Signals:

*	Inflo – Rigid methodology, weak US content, no integrated financials, overwhelming UI.
*	Datasnipper – Price fatigue and performance issues at scale.

We’ve built strong momentum. Excited to roll up my sleeves with all of you to deliver!

__________________________...
```

---

## [TO/CC] 2025-05-23 13:28 | Product Leadership to Deliver the Global AI-Powered Platform of Choice
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

 

For nearly 25 years, Simon Warren has played an extraordinary role in shaping Caseware’s product, people, and presence across the UK and beyond. His influence can be seen not just in what we’ve built, but in how we work — with integrity, with purpose, and with an unwavering commitment to our customers.

 

Today, I’m excited to share that Simon will be stepping into a new enablement leadership role on our Commercial team — while continuing as Managing Director, UK. As you heard from Davis Jackson in our Company Kickoff, sales enablement represents a critical dependency for Caseware to successfully re-ignite growth.  Simon brings a powerful blend of product, domain, and commercial expertise to drive our enablement initiatives with particular focus on our Big Bet 4 (Share and Scale Globally). In addition, he will remain directly engaged in shaping our Financial Reporting roadmap, ensuring strong alignment between our domain leadership and market execution.

 

Please join me in thanking Simon for his leadership — and in supporting him in this next chapter as he continues to shape Caseware’s global impact.  Davis and the Commercial leadership team will be sharing more about Simon’s priority areas of focus. Simon will begin transitioning into his new role through June and July, with a full handover completed by July 31. 

____________________________________________________________________________________ 

 

At our Company Kick-off, we aligned on a bold vision: 

To power trust in the global economy, we must deliver the Global, AI-Powered Platform of Choice for Accounting Professionals. 

 

Today, we’re aligning our product leadership to that strategy — through five execution pillars, each grounded in customer need, commercial opportunity, and tight cross-functional collaboration across Product, Development, and GTM.

 

We’re also making three key leadership shifts to accelerate delivery, effective this Monday, May 26:

 

*	Don van Zijtveld is promoted to...
```

Team,

Our Playbook Is Live. NPO Support Is Live in Canada. CWX Is Live. Let’s Go!

📘 1. Rev 1 — Caseware Product Playbook is Live

This is the rollout.
Today, we officially launch  Rev 1 of the Caseware Product Playbook <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/andrew_smith_caseware_com/EY7qz9z-IAtKh298lUZ1GskBDm3ISGRsX3IHc-0-Vkrv-A?e=Zwffll&&sdata=WThhYVBoNFVzbjFyZjh6NkZ1NVV5N1VJaktBcG4wZm95UzUvT2Q1a0xJWT0%3d>  — our shared operating system for how we build product. It translates our "How We Build" principles — Customer Obsession, Team First, Courage, Ownership, Excellence, Velocity, and Fun— into systematic, scalable practices.

We're starting by focusing on three critical operating mechanisms:

1.	Discovery Reviews – Shape raw problem framing and explore early customer signals
2.	Product Strategy Reviews – Align on investment-worthiness and strategic fit
3.	Experience Reviews – Validate usability, coherence, and design integrity

📅 Next Steps

*	This week: Natasha Menon is aligning on pilot groups with Product Leadership
*	Next week: Kick-offs begin

## [TO/CC] 2025-05-13 00:05 | Re: UK Product Update
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Terrific update! 

Thanks for sharing!

Andrew

Andrew Smith
Chief Product Officer
	
andrew.smith@caseware.com <mailto:andrew.smith@caseware.com> 
	
    
From: Natasha Kobrak <Natasha.Kobrak@caseware.com>
Date: Monday, May 12, 2025 at 6:51 PM
To: teamuk <teamuk@caseware.com>
Cc: Natasha Menon <natasha.menon@caseware.com>, Andrew Smith <andrew.smith@caseware.com>
Subject: UK Product Update

 

 <https://hs-2109885.f.hubspotemail.net/hub/2109885/hubfs/UK_Marketing/Product%20Management%20Team/Product_Update_Email_Banner%201.png?width=1200&upscale=true&name=Product_Update_Email_Banner%201.png> 

In this edition

Shorter edition this month due to a lot of competing priorities. I hope you will all still find it useful!

	

*	On the 14th and 15th of this month is Accountex which is the biggest industry conference of the year. The Stand Messaging this year will be “End-to-end Cloud Solutions for Accounts & Audit” with a particular focus on our recent Audit innovations. Our Cloud Audit solution has taken a huge step up in the last few months and our aim is to showcase its market leading features. These include: 

	*	The addition of many data imports and Validis 
	*	The introduction of Validate
	*	Integrating Analytics into the Audit workflow in conjunction with a sample selector tool in advance of our next stage which will include a deeper integration with Mercia methodology. 
	*	A preview of our cloud engagement trackers to assist with managing and monitoring the progress and workflow of Audit work. 
	*	AiDA as an efficiency driver during fieldwork to help with completing audit procedures in an efficient manner (eg. the summary of operating lease documents etc) and guiding junior employees. 
	*	The new synchronised trial balance functionality which allows a streamlined experience for firms using both cloud Financials and Audit 
	*	Groups functionality 

 

*	This month’s sprint review was on 6th May. All sprint reviews globally have been added to a Caseware Events calendar ...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-05-12 13:14 | Week 12 Product Team Update | LaunchPad, Connection, Execution
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

Last week, we delivered our Platform Roadmap at 100% fidelity. This week, we'll deliver our Solutions Roadmap at 100% fidelity. We’ll then iterate with Commercial to finalize our FY26 Roadmap for June 1.

Importantly, alongside, we're accelerating our focus on Discovery & Execution. And this week also marks something new that helps us build, communicate, and celebrate with clarity.

________________________________

🚀 Caseware LaunchPad Has Launched

Caseware LaunchPad <https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fteams.microsoft.com%2Fl%2Fchannel%2F19%253A9222cf91b67f4cf78b2a520531495b3d%2540thread.tacv2%2FCaseware%2520LaunchPad%3FgroupId%3Deaea9e6f-c0dd-48d0-9bc2-5fb09f0241ab%26tenantId%3D7b8a41e4-2f76-4eef-87d3-f75dd19c0e56&data=05%7C02%7Cliam.bond%40caseware.com%7Cef0f1225637140dc94a008dd914e8880%7C7b8a41e42f764eef87d3f75dd19c0e56%7C0%7C0%7C638826488708457630%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=yTBGW5ufG6eogLWYmktJ774L9RdbrNLH4%2FOdWJYksx4%3D&reserved=0>  is now live — check it out in Microsoft Teams.

It’s our new channel for announcing and celebrating recent launches and releases across the product organization.

Huge thanks to Natasha Mennon for bringing it to life, and to Matt Swann for furnishing the inaugural post <https://casewareonline.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/AllProduct-Team/ERvexGOJ5vBGsmOs53L3q5QBYfzbPWa1qMHoKhCKTvpT9Q?e=a1BjVL&&sdata=cjFLVDNheVR5emxmVERBTGNUT1Z3MlpYbmlOdS9pa0xoZFJpS1lPNDE5Yz0%3d>  on Group Audit.

The objectives are simple:

*	Increase visibility of everyone’s great work

*	Celebrate our wins

*	Stay l...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-05-08 10:09 | Accountex 2025 - information before the event
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Hi everyone, 

 

I hope this message finds you well. 

 

A PowerPoint document has been created and contains important details regarding the event such as opening times, our stand, the floorplan, scanners and the speaker/demo sessions.

You can access the PowerPoint here:  Accountex 2025.pptx <https://casewareonline-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/g/personal/hannah_griffiths_caseware_com/ETGPUai4gQRCg0NA0DhVCUUB0xn6o1FT7XSSeFdbIos77w?e=xzEYSl&&sdata=ZzhYS0ozSUdYLy9sbnFOQVZsQVBUTzZiZ3orVWNOQkd5cUNIUFdVeVpvWT0%3d> 

 

The organisers have provided some key points below that I’d like to share with you all: 

*	NEW Visitor Badge Feature: Please look out for the words ‘Practice’ or ‘Industry’ on the visitor badges.  The visitors can select one of these options when they register.  These are then included on badges to help exhibitors identify if they are relevant to them or not.  
*	Wellbeing Retreat: Our wellness retreat is located at the front of the show, to the left of the entrance as you come in. The Community Massage project will provide massages in exchange for a charity donation. Get relaxed and ready for the day ahead from 9am!  
*	Water Stations & Café: This year, we’ve introduced water stations, sponsored by Intuit QuickBooks, where you can refill your bottles. One station is located near the entrance, and another is between Theatre 3 & 4. Our main café, located at the back of the hall Quickbites and Brews café – Open from 8am, Coffee Grab and Go – Open from 9am, Food Truck – Open from 10am.
*	Accountex Exhibitor Drinks:  We would love you to join us for our famous exhibitor drinks at the Novotel Hotel Bar on Day 1 (14th May) from 6:15pm. Bring your team and c...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-05-05 13:07 | Week 11 Product Team Update | Platform, Rigor, Velocity
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

We're in the final sprint to lock our FY26 Roadmap. This week’s update focuses on who's joined to help us win, what matters most right now, and an early customer signal from the market.

________________________________

Welcome Aichin Chung – And Welcome AI Acceleration

Aichin joins as VP of Product, Core Platform & Intelligence, bringing a rare blend of product and engineering leadership across AI, data, and developer enablement.

At Thomson Reuters, she was VP of Engineering, Enablement & Developer Experience, where she led the rollout of an AI coding assistant that boosted developer productivity by 46% and improved code quality by 39%. Earlier at IBM, she spent nearly a decade driving product strategy for Watson Analytics, Business Analytics, and Hybrid Cloud.

Aichin will lead our Core Platform product teams—including Cloud, Hybrid Cloud, Desktop, and the Muisca & Dacia backend squads—and will accelerate our AI execution rhythm. Vijay and Ricky will each report to Aichin, and she will partner closely with our engineering leaders to define and accelerate the product capabilities, platform patterns, and investment priorities needed to make Caseware AI-ready at the platform layer.

________________________________

Welcome Yuning Wang – And Welcome Investment Rigor & Enhanced Velocity

Yuning joins as Senior Technical Program Manager, with a mandate to drive clarity and rigor into our investment decisions — and to unblock dependencies in our most critical initiatives.

She joins from McKinsey & Company, where she was a Senior Business Analyst advising on AI, innovation, and investment strategy. Her experience spans developing the investment thesis and operating model for a $100M+ R&D initiative, leading the rollout of McKinsey’s internal GenAI platform Lilli, and designing procurement strategy changes that unlocked $30M+ in savings.

Yuning will lead execution of our Best-in-Class Product Investment Rigor & ROI input goal, ensuring we scale with discipline...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-04-28 14:56 | Week 10 Product Team Update | Shipping, Delivering, Prioritizing
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

Thank you to everyone who joined our Product Team All Hands last Friday for a walkthrough of Rev 1 of our 3/1Y Product Strategy. This week’s update focuses on three things: (1) a look back at what we’ve shipped over the last four weeks (framed by our draft 3/1Y strategic product pillars), (2) direct feedback on How We're Delivering from one of our largest customers, and (3) our top collective priority for the week ahead.

________________________________

What We Shipped (Last 4 Weeks)

Global Platform 
We delivered a major boost in speed and stability capability for our largest clients. Specifically, following a critical hotfix and new traffic controls, key workflows at Forvis Mazars are now up to four times faster, and overall system efficiency is significantly improved—even as usage grows. Special thanks to Mark Chisholm, Craig Thorvardson, Amir Toole, Mike Gallamore, Mohammad Usman, Sasha Dobrovolskyi, Kartik Narayanan, Vijay Nair and the entire team that made this happen. Enabling global platform scale, intelligence, modularity & coherence will be a critical theme throughout FY26. 

Cloud Adoption
Over the last month, we launched Group Audit to beta, a first-class platform capability that allows users to perform more efficient group audits. This is a significant milestone for our audit solutions globally and will be a major driver of cloud adoption and value as it launches to customers in the coming months. We also enabled new embedded analytics workflows and increased the size of datasets supported—making it easier and more efficient for auditors to access insights. Finally, we completed important extensibility work for our Validis data-in integration (live across US solutions, rolling out elsewhere in May), allowing us to make enhancements to this solution with higher velocity going forward.

US Market Penetration
In addition to accelerated velocity on platform scalability and investments in data-in and analytics efficiency—mentioned above—we shipped T...
```

## [TO/CC] 2025-04-21 13:06 | Week 9 Product Team Update | A Lot on the Go, and Tremendous Progress
**Folder:** \\liam.bond@caseware.com\Deleted Items

```
Team,

We’ve got a lot on the go right now — and I know how hard everyone is working. I appreciate it. Our customers appreciate it even more. Thank you for the tremendous progress.

✅ Rev 1 of our 3/1Y Product Strategy is in place — we’ll continue to iterate as we finalize for Aug 1
✅ Rev 1 Input Goals are set — we’ll evolve them further, anchoring on measurable outcomes that drive value for our customers

This week, we’re rolling up our sleeves for Quarterly Roadmap Planning — translating our strategy into scoping conversations with R&D and aligning with our commercial partners to drive clarity on investment priorities. We'll be delineating:

*	Where we have high confidence and are ready to partner with Engineering to scope the work

*	Where we need to build confidence — and how we partner with our commercial teams to tie product priorities to financial outcomes and make the right trade-offs

Speaking of commercial partnership — a big shout-out to Natasha Menon, Travis Finch, Brian Heath, Amy Fairchild, Phil Wiles, and their teams for the incredible work on our Product GTM Engagement Model. This mission team is mapping current friction points, aligning cross-functional roles, and surfacing early quick wins. This foundational work will empower us to operate as one team.

⏱ Meeting Hygiene Reminder
As a reminder from our Product Team Meeting Impact & Efficiency Principles:
👉 Please send action items promptly after meetings, and follow up on them at the start of the next one.
It’s a simple habit that keeps us aligned, accountable, and moving fast.

💡 Investing in Our Discovery Capabilities
On April 23–24, we’re hosting Product Discovery Training with Product Faculty (virtually). Jennifer Liu — who led product at Google, Ethos Life, and Lattice — will be our core faculty member. We’re also thrilled to welcome two guest faculty leaders:

Day 1: Amit Fulay, VP of Product at Microsoft
Day 2: Jon Alferness, EVP at Walmart and former VP of Product at Google

This training...
```

Subject: Lead schedules - Mark Grrney and Dains frustration 
 
Hi Jon,

I had a lovely call with Mark this afternoon - overall he was positive about cloud audit but had a question for you, he asked why we've set the LS up how we have on the Charity Cloud Audit app.

The LS on the charity audit app has a summary and then numerous sub LS showing individual asset classes. More often than not those sub LS are blank as they are not relevant classes, it takes time to review blank content before sign off, and it's not consistent with the company audit template. Mark said there should be a simple multi column LS, with a column for each class of assets that are relevant to the client file - exactly like we created it on the desktop. 

Our Charity ...

I want to go back to Tony with some accurate wording on why we haven't completed all the lead schedules in the charity template. I know it is because we just haven't had the time to really spec them out with the different mapping and on the analysis side of things the challenge has been in respect of being able to get an accurate determination on where the different codes should go. But I could do with something more comprehensive from when the template was set up in the first place.

I'm actually open to working with any user to get these written in due course, but they have to remain a low priority for the time being.
